---
title: '`r params$target_sector`: Technology Adoption and Productivity Analysis'
subtitle: "Vietnam Enterprise Survey: 34 CGE Sectors, 2019-2023 | Real 2019 VND"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    df_print: paged
  pdf_document:
    toc: false
    toc_depth: '2'
params:
  target_sector: "Education"
  data_path: 'dn18_23_Cleaning&SectorAggregation_FINAL.rds'
  decomp_path: 'LP_decomposition_CGEsector.xlsx'
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo      = FALSE,
  warning   = FALSE,
  message   = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center",
  fig.width = 11,
  fig.height = 6.5
)

library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(glue)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(sf)
library(geodata)
library(stringi)
library(purrr)
library(splines)
library(quantreg)
library(readxl)

sf::sf_use_s2(FALSE)

base_year      <- 2019
snapshot_year  <- 2023
target_sector  <- params$target_sector
analysis_years <- 2019:2023

# ── Data loading ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# The template looks for an object called df_final in the R environment first.
# If not found, it reads from params$data_path. The default relative path
# "dn18_23_Cleaning&SectorAggregation_FINAL.rds" assumes the .rds file sits in
# the same folder as this .Rmd. Adjust params$data_path if it is elsewhere.
if (!exists("df_final")) {
  if (!is.null(params$data_path) && params$data_path != "") {
    df_final <- readr::read_rds(params$data_path)
  } else {
    stop(
      "df_final is not loaded. Supply params$data_path pointing to ",
      "dn18_23_Cleaning&SectorAggregation_FINAL.rds, or load df_final ",
      "into your R session before rendering."
    )
  }
}

# ── Helper functions ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

safe_divide <- function(numerator, denominator) {
  ifelse(is.na(denominator) | denominator == 0, NA_real_, numerator / denominator)
}

# Returns a colour-coded HTML span with a directional arrow and percentage.
format_change <- function(x) {
  arrow  <- ifelse(x >= 0, "↑", "↓")
  colour <- ifelse(x >= 0, "#15803D", "#DC2626")
  sign   <- ifelse(x >= 0, "+", "")
  paste0(
    "<span style='color:", colour, "; font-weight:700;'>",
    arrow, " ", sign, percent(x, accuracy = 0.1),
    "</span>"
  )
}

# Returns a plain-English description of growth magnitude.
growth_strength <- function(x) {
  case_when(
    is.na(x)   ~ "showed limited measurable change",
    x >= 0.20  ~ "increased strongly",
    x >= 0.05  ~ "increased moderately",
    x > -0.05  ~ "remained broadly stable",
    x > -0.20  ~ "declined moderately",
    TRUE        ~ "declined strongly"
  )
}

# Strips accents and non-alphanumeric characters for province name matching.
clean_name <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    stringi::stri_trans_general("Latin-ASCII") %>%
    str_to_lower() %>%
    str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9]", "")
}

# Wraps interpretation text in a styled left-bordered div for consistent
# visual formatting across all sections.
interp <- function(...) {
  text <- paste0(...)
  text <- gsub("\\*\\*(.*?)\\*\\*", "<strong>\\1</strong>", text, perl = TRUE)
  cat(paste0('<div class="interpretation">\n\n', text, '\n\n</div>\n'))
}

theme_sector <- function() {
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, colour = "#12355B"),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10.5, colour = "#5B6F8A", lineheight = 1.25),
      axis.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position  = "bottom",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.margin      = margin(10, 15, 10, 15)
    )
}
```

```{r css, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
cat("
<style>

/* ── Google Font (Inter) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:ital,wght@0,300;0,400;0,600;0,700;1,400&display=swap');

/* ── Base typography ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
body {
  font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 1.70;
  color: #1F2933;
}

/* ── Headings ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
h1 {
  color: #12355B;
  font-weight: 700;
  border-bottom: 3px solid #2A9D8F;
  padding-bottom: 10px;
  margin-top: 48px;
  margin-bottom: 6px;
}
h2 {
  color: #12355B;
  font-weight: 600;
  font-size: 1.15em;
  border-left: 4px solid #2A9D8F;
  padding-left: 12px;
  margin-top: 32px;
  margin-bottom: 4px;
}
h3 { color: #12355B; font-weight: 600; }

/* ── One-line section description below each heading ─────────────────────── */
.section-desc {
  color: #5B6F8A;
  font-size: 13.5px;
  margin: -4px 0 16px 0;
  font-style: italic;
}

/* ── Interpretation block ────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
.interpretation {
  background: #EDF4FB;
  border-left: 4px solid #2A9D8F;
  padding: 14px 18px 12px 18px;
  border-radius: 0 6px 6px 0;
  margin: 12px 0 24px 0;
  font-size: 14px;
  line-height: 1.75;
}
.interpretation::before {
  content: 'ANALYTICAL NOTE';
  display: block;
  font-size: 10px;
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: 1.5px;
  color: #2A9D8F;
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}

/* ── Data quality callout ────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
.data-note {
  background: #FFFBF0;
  border-left: 4px solid #F4A261;
  padding: 10px 14px;
  border-radius: 0 6px 6px 0;
  margin: 8px 0 16px 0;
  font-size: 13px;
  color: #334E68;
}

/* ── Readme box ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
.readme-box {
  background: #F8F9FA;
  border: 1px solid #D0DAE6;
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 18px 22px;
  margin-bottom: 28px;
  font-size: 14px;
}
.readme-box pre { background: #E8ECEF; padding: 10px 14px; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 13px; }
.readme-box code { font-size: 13px; }

/* ── Misc ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
.caption { color: #6B7280; font-size: 12px; }
.chart-card {
  padding: 16px 18px;
  border: 1px solid #E5E7EB;
  border-radius: 12px;
  margin-bottom: 22px;
  background: #FFFFFF;
}

/* ── Cover block ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
.cover-block {
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #12355B 0%, #1a4a7a 100%);
  color: #FFFFFF;
  padding: 36px 40px 32px 40px;
  border-radius: 10px;
  margin-bottom: 36px;
}
.cover-block .sector-name {
  font-size: 26px;
  font-weight: 700;
  line-height: 1.3;
  margin-bottom: 6px;
}
.cover-block .cover-subtitle {
  font-size: 14px;
  color: #A8C7E8;
  margin-bottom: 20px;
  letter-spacing: 0.3px;
}
.cover-block .cover-meta {
  display: flex;
  gap: 32px;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  border-top: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2);
  padding-top: 16px;
}
.cover-block .meta-item {
  font-size: 12px;
  color: #C8DCF0;
}
.cover-block .meta-item strong {
  display: block;
  color: #FFFFFF;
  font-size: 13px;
  margin-bottom: 2px;
}

/* ── Part divider ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
.part-divider {
  border-top: 2px solid #2A9D8F;
  margin: 52px 0 36px 0;
  text-align: center;
  position: relative;
}
.part-divider span {
  background: white;
  padding: 0 18px;
  color: #5B6F8A;
  font-size: 11px;
  font-weight: 600;
  letter-spacing: 1.8px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  position: relative;
  top: -10px;
  font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif;
}

/* ── Figure captions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
.figure .caption, caption {
  font-size: 12.5px;
  color: #5B6F8A;
  font-style: italic;
  text-align: left;
  margin-top: 6px;
  line-height: 1.5;
}


/* ── Section 4 insight blocks ─────────────────────────────────────────────── */
.highlight-grid{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(260px,1fr));gap:14px;margin:16px 0 24px 0;}
.highlight-block{border:1px solid #D9E1E8;border-top:4px solid #2A9D8F;border-radius:8px;padding:14px 16px;background:#fff;box-shadow:0 1px 4px rgba(18,53,91,.06);}
.highlight-title{display:block;font-weight:700;color:#12355B;margin-bottom:6px;font-size:14px;}
.highlight-body{color:#334E68;font-size:13.5px;line-height:1.65;}
.policy-box{background:#F8FAFC;border-left:4px solid #12355B;border-radius:0 8px 8px 0;padding:16px 18px;margin:14px 0 20px 0;font-size:14px;line-height:1.75;}
.policy-box strong{color:#12355B;}
.strategy-close{background:linear-gradient(135deg,#F1F7F9 0%,#fff 100%);border:1px solid #D9E1E8;border-radius:10px;padding:18px 22px;margin:16px 0 28px 0;color:#12355B;font-size:15px;line-height:1.75;}

/* ── Print styles ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
@media print {
  .cover-block { -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; print-color-adjust: exact; }
  body { font-size: 12px; line-height: 1.55; }
  h1 { font-size: 18px; margin-top: 24px; }
  h2 { font-size: 14px; }
  .interpretation { break-inside: avoid; }
  img { max-width: 100%; page-break-inside: avoid; }
  .part-divider { margin: 24px 0 18px 0; }
}

</style>
")
```

```{=html}
<!--
HOW TO RUN THIS REPORT
======================

Dataset:
  Place "dn18_23_Cleaning&SectorAggregation_FINAL.rds" in the same folder as
  this .Rmd, or update params$data_path to the correct absolute path.

Required R packages:
  tidyverse, scales, glue, knitr, kableExtra, sf, geodata, stringi, purrr

Option 1 - Knit interactively in RStudio:
  Click Knit -> Knit with Parameters, type the sector name in the dialog,
  and click Knit. The sector name must exactly match a CGE_sectorname value
  in the dataset.

Option 2 - Render from the R console:
  rmarkdown::render(
    input         = "sector_report_template_complete.Rmd",
    params        = list(target_sector = "ICT and publishing"),
    output_file   = "output/ICT_and_publishing_report.html",
    knit_root_dir = getwd()
  )

Note: A batch render script for all 34 CGE sectors will be provided separately
after this template is validated on individual sectors.
-->
```

```{r data-preparation, include=FALSE}
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Filter to target sector
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

sector_df <- df_final %>%
  filter(CGE_sectorname == target_sector)

if (nrow(sector_df) == 0) {
  stop(glue(
    "No data found for target_sector = '{target_sector}'. ",
    "Check that the name exactly matches a CGE_sectorname value in the dataset."
  ))
}

df_page1 <- df_final

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# sector_year: one row per survey year for the target sector.
# All monetary values are already in real million VND (2019 base).
# Intensity metrics use ratio-of-totals (sum numerator / sum denominator)
# to avoid giving equal weight to large and small firms.
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

sector_year <- sector_df %>%
  group_by(survey_year) %>%
  summarise(
    firms                              = n_distinct(tax_id),
    labour                             = sum(labour_headcount_end, na.rm = TRUE),
    female_labour                      = sum(labour_female_headcount_end, na.rm = TRUE),
    revenue                            = sum(total_net_revenue, na.rm = TRUE),
    capital                            = sum(capital_sources_total_end, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_investment                   = sum(total_investment, na.rm = TRUE),
    equipment_investment               = sum(investment_machinery_equipment, na.rm = TRUE),
    innovation_investment              = sum(innovation_investment_proxy, na.rm = TRUE),
    firms_with_any_selected_investment = n_distinct(tax_id[has_equipment_investment == 1 | has_innovation_investment == 1]),
    equipment_adoption_rate            = safe_divide(n_distinct(tax_id[has_equipment_investment == 1]), n_distinct(tax_id)),
    innovation_adoption_rate           = safe_divide(n_distinct(tax_id[has_innovation_investment == 1]), n_distinct(tax_id)),
    female_labour_share                = safe_divide(female_labour, labour),
    labour_productivity                = safe_divide(revenue, labour),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# all_sector_year: same structure but for every CGE sector, used for benchmarks.
all_sector_year <- df_final %>%
  group_by(CGE_sectorname, survey_year) %>%
  summarise(
    firms               = n_distinct(tax_id),
    labour              = sum(labour_headcount_end, na.rm = TRUE),
    revenue             = sum(total_net_revenue, na.rm = TRUE),
    labour_productivity = safe_divide(revenue, labour),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Firm dynamics: entry, exit, and staying counts.
# Used in the Page 1 KPI table and Visual 3.
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

firm_year_base <- sector_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(tax_id), !is.na(survey_year)) %>%
  distinct(tax_id, survey_year)

all_years <- sort(unique(firm_year_base$survey_year))

firm_flow_data <- map_dfr(all_years, function(y) {
  current_firms  <- firm_year_base %>% filter(survey_year == y)     %>% pull(tax_id) %>% unique()
  previous_firms <- firm_year_base %>% filter(survey_year == y - 1) %>% pull(tax_id) %>% unique()
  tibble(
    survey_year        = y,
    staying            = ifelse(y == min(all_years), length(current_firms), length(intersect(current_firms, previous_firms))),
    entry              = ifelse(y == min(all_years), 0, length(setdiff(current_firms, previous_firms))),
    exit               = ifelse(y == min(all_years), 0, length(setdiff(previous_firms, current_firms))),
    total_active_firms = length(current_firms)
  )
})

output_index_data <- sector_df %>%
  group_by(survey_year) %>%
  summarise(total_output = sum(total_net_revenue, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(survey_year) %>%
  mutate(
    output_base  = total_output[survey_year == base_year][1],
    output_index = safe_divide(total_output, output_base) * 100
  ) %>%
  select(survey_year, output_index)

firm_flow_data <- firm_flow_data %>%
  left_join(output_index_data, by = "survey_year") %>%
  arrange(survey_year)

kpi_firm_flow_snapshot <- firm_flow_data %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
  transmute(
    staying_firms           = staying,
    entry_firms             = entry,
    exit_firms              = exit,
    staying_rate            = safe_divide(staying, total_active_firms),
    entry_rate              = safe_divide(entry, total_active_firms),
    exit_rate               = safe_divide(exit, staying + exit),
    sector_switch_out_firms = 0,
    sector_switch_out_rate  = 0
  )

bar_positive <- firm_flow_data %>%
  select(survey_year, staying, entry) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(staying, entry), names_to = "Firm flow", values_to = "Number of firms") %>%
  mutate(
    `Firm flow` = recode(`Firm flow`, staying = "Staying (incumbents)", entry = "Entry"),
    x_pos = as.numeric(factor(survey_year)) - 0.16
  )

bar_exit <- firm_flow_data %>%
  transmute(
    survey_year,
    `Firm flow`       = "Exit",
    `Number of firms` = -exit,
    x_pos             = as.numeric(factor(survey_year)) + 0.22
  )

firm_axis_max   <- max(firm_flow_data$staying + firm_flow_data$entry, na.rm = TRUE)
firm_axis_min   <- -max(firm_flow_data$exit, na.rm = TRUE)
index_min       <- min(firm_flow_data$output_index, na.rm = TRUE)
index_max       <- max(firm_flow_data$output_index, na.rm = TRUE)
index_padding   <- (index_max - index_min) * 0.10
index_min_plot  <- index_min - index_padding
index_max_plot  <- index_max + index_padding
scale_factor_v3 <- ifelse(
  index_max_plot > index_min_plot,
  (firm_axis_max - firm_axis_min) / (index_max_plot - index_min_plot),
  1
)
```

::: part-divider
<span>Part I: Sector Structure and Performance</span>
:::

# 1. Sector Structure and Performance

## 1.1 KPI Snapshot

<p class="section-desc">

Key performance indicators for `r snapshot_year` compared with the `r base_year` baseline, covering firm count, employment, revenue scale, capital intensity, and firm entry/exit dynamics.

</p>

```{r page1-kpi, results='asis'}
kpi_base <- sector_year %>%
  filter(survey_year == base_year) %>%
  summarise(
    firms_base                 = sum(firms, na.rm = TRUE),
    labour_base                = sum(labour, na.rm = TRUE),
    revenue_base               = sum(revenue, na.rm = TRUE),
    female_labour_share_base   = safe_divide(sum(female_labour, na.rm = TRUE), sum(labour, na.rm = TRUE)),
    revenue_per_firm_base      = safe_divide(sum(revenue, na.rm = TRUE), sum(firms, na.rm = TRUE)),
    capital_per_worker_base    = safe_divide(sum(capital, na.rm = TRUE), sum(labour, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

kpi_snapshot_raw <- sector_year %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
  summarise(
    firms                = sum(firms, na.rm = TRUE),
    labour               = sum(labour, na.rm = TRUE),
    revenue              = sum(revenue, na.rm = TRUE),
    female_labour_share  = safe_divide(sum(female_labour, na.rm = TRUE), sum(labour, na.rm = TRUE)),
    revenue_per_firm     = safe_divide(sum(revenue, na.rm = TRUE), sum(firms, na.rm = TRUE)),
    capital_per_worker   = safe_divide(sum(capital, na.rm = TRUE), sum(labour, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  bind_cols(kpi_base) %>%
  bind_cols(kpi_firm_flow_snapshot) %>%
  mutate(
    firms_growth               = safe_divide(firms, firms_base) - 1,
    labour_growth              = safe_divide(labour, labour_base) - 1,
    revenue_growth             = safe_divide(revenue, revenue_base) - 1,
    female_labour_share_change = female_labour_share - female_labour_share_base,
    revenue_per_firm_growth    = safe_divide(revenue_per_firm, revenue_per_firm_base) - 1,
    capital_per_worker_growth  = safe_divide(capital_per_worker, capital_per_worker_base) - 1
  )

kpi_table <- tibble(
  `Left KPI` = c("Active firms", "Total labour", "Average revenue per firm", "Firm dynamics"),
  `Left Value` = c(
    paste0(comma(kpi_snapshot_raw$firms), " firms (", format_change(kpi_snapshot_raw$firms_growth), " vs 2019)"),
    paste0(comma(kpi_snapshot_raw$labour), " workers (", format_change(kpi_snapshot_raw$labour_growth), " vs 2019)"),
    paste0(comma(round(kpi_snapshot_raw$revenue_per_firm, 1)), " mn VND/firm (", format_change(kpi_snapshot_raw$revenue_per_firm_growth), " vs 2019)"),
    paste0("Stay: ", comma(kpi_snapshot_raw$staying_firms), " (", percent(kpi_snapshot_raw$staying_rate, accuracy = 0.1), ")", " | Entry: ", comma(kpi_snapshot_raw$entry_firms), " (", percent(kpi_snapshot_raw$entry_rate, accuracy = 0.1), ")")
  ),
  `Right KPI` = c("Total revenue", "Female labour share", "Capital Intensity", "Firm dynamics"),
  `Right Value` = c(
    paste0(comma(round(kpi_snapshot_raw$revenue / 1e6, 2)), " trillion VND (", format_change(kpi_snapshot_raw$revenue_growth), " vs 2019)"),
    paste0(percent(kpi_snapshot_raw$female_labour_share, accuracy = 0.1), " (", format_change(kpi_snapshot_raw$female_labour_share_change), " percentage points vs 2019)"),
    paste0(comma(round(kpi_snapshot_raw$capital_per_worker, 1)), " mn VND/worker (", format_change(kpi_snapshot_raw$capital_per_worker_growth), " vs 2019)"),
    paste0("Exit: ", comma(kpi_snapshot_raw$exit_firms), " (", percent(kpi_snapshot_raw$exit_rate, accuracy = 0.1), ")", " | Switch-out: ", comma(kpi_snapshot_raw$sector_switch_out_firms), " (", percent(kpi_snapshot_raw$sector_switch_out_rate, accuracy = 0.1), ")")
  )
)

kpi_table %>%
  kable(
    format = "html", escape = FALSE,
    caption = glue("{target_sector}: Page 1 KPI Snapshot, {snapshot_year}"),
    col.names = c("", "", "", "")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = TRUE,
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    font_size = 14
  ) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE, color = "#12355B", width = "20%", extra_css = "white-space: nowrap;") %>%
  column_spec(2, color = "#1F2933", width = "28%") %>%
  column_spec(3, bold = TRUE, color = "#12355B", width = "20%", extra_css = "white-space: nowrap;") %>%
  column_spec(4, color = "#1F2933", width = "32%") %>%
  row_spec(4, extra_css = "border-top: 2px solid #D9E1E8;")
```

```{r page1-kpi-interpretation, results='asis'}
net_firm_change <- kpi_snapshot_raw$entry_firms - kpi_snapshot_raw$exit_firms
net_direction   <- if_else(net_firm_change >= 0, "net gain", "net loss")
scale_msg       <- if_else(kpi_snapshot_raw$revenue_per_firm_growth >= 0, "improving", "deteriorating")

interp(glue(
  "**{snapshot_year} snapshot:** {target_sector} comprised **{comma(kpi_snapshot_raw$firms)}** active firms generating ",
  "**{comma(round(kpi_snapshot_raw$revenue / 1e6, 2))} trillion VND** in real revenue ",
  "({format_change(kpi_snapshot_raw$revenue_growth)} vs {base_year}), employing **{comma(kpi_snapshot_raw$labour)}** workers ",
  "({format_change(kpi_snapshot_raw$labour_growth)} vs {base_year}). Average revenue per firm stood at ",
  "**{comma(round(kpi_snapshot_raw$revenue_per_firm, 1))} million VND** ({format_change(kpi_snapshot_raw$revenue_per_firm_growth)} vs {base_year}), ",
  "indicating {scale_msg} firm-level scale. ",
  "**Firm dynamics:** **{comma(kpi_snapshot_raw$entry_firms)}** firms entered and **{comma(kpi_snapshot_raw$exit_firms)}** exited ",
  "in {snapshot_year}, a {net_direction} of **{comma(abs(net_firm_change))}** firms. ",
  "**{comma(kpi_snapshot_raw$staying_firms)}** firms ({percent(kpi_snapshot_raw$staying_rate, accuracy = 0.1)}) ",
  "were continuing incumbents."
))
```

## 1.2 Revenue Scale and Labour Benchmark

<p class="section-desc">

Sector total revenue and labour headcount compared against the all-sector average, `r base_year`–`r snapshot_year`. Left axis: revenue (trillion VND). Right axis: labour headcount.

</p>

```{r page1-visual1-prep, include=FALSE}
labour_selected_label  <- glue("{target_sector} Labour")
labour_average_label   <- "Average Labour - All Sectors"
selected_revenue_label <- glue("{target_sector} Sector Revenue")
average_revenue_label  <- "Average Revenue - All Sectors"

all_sector_benchmark <- all_sector_year %>%
  group_by(survey_year) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_sector_revenue = mean(revenue, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_sector_labour  = mean(labour, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

v1_sector <- sector_year %>%
  left_join(all_sector_benchmark, by = "survey_year") %>%
  arrange(survey_year) %>%
  transmute(
    survey_year,
    !!selected_revenue_label := revenue,
    !!average_revenue_label  := mean_sector_revenue,
    !!labour_selected_label  := labour,
    !!labour_average_label   := mean_sector_labour
  )

v1_bar <- v1_sector %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(all_of(selected_revenue_label), all_of(average_revenue_label)),
    names_to = "Revenue benchmark", values_to = "Revenue"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    revenue_trillion    = Revenue / 1e6,
    `Revenue benchmark` = factor(`Revenue benchmark`, levels = c(selected_revenue_label, average_revenue_label))
  )

v1_line <- v1_sector %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(all_of(labour_selected_label), all_of(labour_average_label)),
    names_to = "Labour benchmark", values_to = "Labour"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    `Labour benchmark` = factor(`Labour benchmark`, levels = c(labour_selected_label, labour_average_label))
  )

max_revenue    <- max(v1_bar$revenue_trillion, na.rm = TRUE)
max_labour     <- max(v1_line$Labour, na.rm = TRUE)
scale_factor_v1 <- ifelse(max_labour > 0, max_revenue / max_labour, 1)
```

```{r page1-visual1, fig.cap=paste0('Figure 1.1: Total sector revenue and labour headcount benchmarked against the all-sector average, ', base_year, '–', snapshot_year, '.')}
ggplot() +
  geom_col(
    data = v1_bar,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = revenue_trillion, fill = `Revenue benchmark`),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7), width = 0.6, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.35
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = v1_line,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = Labour * scale_factor_v1, colour = `Labour benchmark`, group = `Labour benchmark`),
    linewidth = 1.25
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = v1_line,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = Labour * scale_factor_v1, colour = `Labour benchmark`, shape = `Labour benchmark`),
    size = 3
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = v1_bar,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = revenue_trillion, label = comma(round(revenue_trillion, 2)),
        group = `Revenue benchmark`),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    vjust = -0.45, size = 3, fontface = "bold", colour = "#12355B"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name   = "Revenue (trillion VND)",
    labels = comma_format(accuracy = 0.01),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)),
    sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . / scale_factor_v1, name = "Labour headcount", labels = comma_format(accuracy = 1))
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = setNames(c("#2A9D8F", "#D9E1E8"), c(selected_revenue_label, average_revenue_label)),
    name = "Revenue benchmark", drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = setNames(c("#E76F51", "#6B7280"), c(labour_selected_label, labour_average_label)),
    name = "Labour benchmark", drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = setNames(c(16, 15), c(labour_selected_label, labour_average_label)),
    name = "Labour benchmark"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = glue("{target_sector}: Revenue Scale and Labour Benchmark"),
    subtitle = paste(
      glue("Bars compare {target_sector} revenue with average all-sector revenue; lines compare total labour headcount"),
      "\nLeft axis measures revenue (trillion VND). Right axis measures labour headcount."
    ),
    x = NULL
  ) +
  guides(
    fill   = guide_legend(order = 1),
    colour = guide_legend(order = 2, override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.3, size = 3)),
    shape  = guide_legend(order = 2)
  ) +
  theme_sector() +
  theme(
    legend.position      = "bottom",
    legend.box           = "vertical",
    legend.title         = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y.left    = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#2A9D8F"),
    axis.text.y.left     = element_text(colour = "#2A9D8F"),
    axis.title.y.right   = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#E76F51"),
    axis.text.y.right    = element_text(colour = "#E76F51")
  )
```

```{r page1-visual1-interpretation, results='asis'}
v1_stats <- sector_year %>%
  filter(survey_year %in% c(base_year, snapshot_year)) %>%
  summarise(
    revenue_growth = safe_divide(revenue[survey_year == snapshot_year], revenue[survey_year == base_year]) - 1,
    labour_growth  = safe_divide(labour[survey_year == snapshot_year], labour[survey_year == base_year]) - 1
  )

rev_2019        <- sector_year %>% filter(survey_year == base_year) %>% pull(revenue)
rev_2023        <- sector_year %>% filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>% pull(revenue)
benchmark_2019  <- all_sector_benchmark %>% filter(survey_year == base_year) %>% pull(mean_sector_revenue)
benchmark_2023  <- all_sector_benchmark %>% filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>% pull(mean_sector_revenue)
rev_ratio       <- safe_divide(rev_2023, benchmark_2023)
rev_ratio_base  <- safe_divide(rev_2019, benchmark_2019)
rev_ratio_msg   <- if_else(
  rev_ratio >= 1,
  glue("{round(rev_ratio, 1)}x the all-sector average"),
  glue("{percent(rev_ratio, accuracy = 1)} of the all-sector average")
)

benchmark_gap_msg <- case_when(
  is.na(rev_ratio_base) | is.na(rev_ratio) ~ "",
  rev_ratio > rev_ratio_base + 0.2 ~ glue(
    "The sector's revenue advantage over the all-sector average widened ",
    "(from {round(rev_ratio_base, 1)}x in {base_year} to {round(rev_ratio, 1)}x in {snapshot_year}), ",
    "indicating that the sector expanded its relative economic footprint."
  ),
  rev_ratio < rev_ratio_base - 0.2 ~ glue(
    "The sector's revenue position relative to the all-sector average narrowed ",
    "(from {round(rev_ratio_base, 1)}x in {base_year} to {round(rev_ratio, 1)}x in {snapshot_year}), ",
    "suggesting other sectors grew faster over the period."
  ),
  TRUE ~ "The sector's revenue position relative to the all-sector average remained broadly stable across the period."
)

revenue_labour_msg <- case_when(
  v1_stats$revenue_growth > v1_stats$labour_growth + 0.05 ~
    "Revenue grew materially faster than employment, consistent with improving revenue intensity and rising labour productivity at the sector level.",
  v1_stats$labour_growth > v1_stats$revenue_growth + 0.05 ~
    "Employment expanded faster than revenue, suggesting the sector absorbed labour without equivalent productivity gains over the period.",
  TRUE ~
    "Revenue and employment grew at broadly similar rates, indicating stable revenue per worker rather than productivity improvement."
)

interp(glue(
  "Between {base_year} and {snapshot_year}, {target_sector} total revenue {growth_strength(v1_stats$revenue_growth)} ",
  "({format_change(v1_stats$revenue_growth)}), reaching **{comma(round(rev_2023 / 1e6, 2))} trillion VND**, ",
  "which is {rev_ratio_msg} in {snapshot_year}. ",
  "{benchmark_gap_msg} ",
  "Employment {growth_strength(v1_stats$labour_growth)} ({format_change(v1_stats$labour_growth)}) over the same period. ",
  "{revenue_labour_msg}"
))
```

## 1.3 Five-Year Revenue Share by Ownership Type

<p class="section-desc">

Cumulative revenue share by ownership type (SOE, domestic private, FDI) pooled across `r base_year`–`r snapshot_year`, benchmarked against the all-sector economy.

</p>

```{r page1-visual2-prep, include=FALSE}
ownership_palette_benchmark <- c("Selected sector" = "#2A9D8F", "All sectors" = "#D9E1E8")
ownership_levels <- c("SOE", "Domestic private firms", "FDI")

sector_ownership_share_5yr <- sector_df %>%
  filter(survey_year %in% analysis_years, !is.na(firm_ownership_type_factor)) %>%
  mutate(Ownership = factor(as.character(firm_ownership_type_factor), levels = ownership_levels)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Ownership)) %>%
  group_by(Ownership) %>%
  summarise(revenue = sum(total_net_revenue, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  tidyr::complete(Ownership = factor(ownership_levels, levels = ownership_levels), fill = list(revenue = 0)) %>%
  mutate(revenue_share = revenue / sum(revenue, na.rm = TRUE), Benchmark = "Selected sector")

all_sector_ownership_share_5yr <- df_final %>%
  filter(survey_year %in% analysis_years, !is.na(firm_ownership_type_factor)) %>%
  mutate(Ownership = factor(as.character(firm_ownership_type_factor), levels = ownership_levels)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Ownership)) %>%
  group_by(Ownership) %>%
  summarise(revenue = sum(total_net_revenue, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  tidyr::complete(Ownership = factor(ownership_levels, levels = ownership_levels), fill = list(revenue = 0)) %>%
  mutate(revenue_share = revenue / sum(revenue, na.rm = TRUE), Benchmark = "All sectors")

ownership_grouped_data <- bind_rows(sector_ownership_share_5yr, all_sector_ownership_share_5yr) %>%
  mutate(
    Ownership = factor(Ownership, levels = ownership_levels),
    Benchmark = factor(Benchmark, levels = c("Selected sector", "All sectors")),
    bar_label = percent(revenue_share, accuracy = 0.1)
  )
```

```{r page1-visual2, fig.cap=paste0('Figure 1.2: Five-year cumulative revenue share by ownership type (SOE, domestic private, FDI), ', base_year, '–', snapshot_year, ', benchmarked against all sectors.')}
ggplot(ownership_grouped_data, aes(x = Ownership, y = revenue_share, fill = Benchmark)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.72), width = 0.62, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = bar_label),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.72), vjust = -0.35,
    size = 3.8, fontface = "bold", colour = "#12355B"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(ownership_grouped_data$revenue_share, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.18),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ownership_palette_benchmark, name = "Benchmark") +
  labs(
    title    = glue("{target_sector}: Five-Year Revenue Share by Ownership Type"),
    subtitle = "Revenue share by ownership type across 2019–2023, compared with all sectors",
    x = NULL, y = "Revenue share"
  ) +
  theme_sector() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "#E6EAF0", linewidth = 0.35),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#12355B", size = 11),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(colour = "#5B6F8A", size = 10),
    legend.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 10, colour = "#5B6F8A"),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 20, 10, 20)
  )
```

```{r page1-visual2-interpretation, results='asis'}
ownership_ranked <- ownership_grouped_data %>%
  filter(Benchmark == "Selected sector") %>%
  arrange(desc(revenue_share))

dominant_owner <- ownership_ranked %>% slice(1)
second_owner   <- ownership_ranked %>% slice(2)

all_dominant <- ownership_grouped_data %>%
  filter(Benchmark == "All sectors", Ownership == dominant_owner$Ownership) %>%
  pull(revenue_share)
all_second <- ownership_grouped_data %>%
  filter(Benchmark == "All sectors", Ownership == second_owner$Ownership) %>%
  pull(revenue_share)

gap_dominant <- dominant_owner$revenue_share - all_dominant
gap_second   <- second_owner$revenue_share - all_second

gap_dominant_msg <- if_else(
  gap_dominant > 0,
  glue("{percent(gap_dominant, accuracy = 0.1)} percentage points above the all-sector benchmark"),
  glue("{percent(abs(gap_dominant), accuracy = 0.1)} percentage points below the all-sector benchmark")
)
gap_second_dir <- if_else(gap_second >= 0, "above", "below")

structural_ownership_msg <- case_when(
  as.character(dominant_owner$Ownership) == "FDI" & dominant_owner$revenue_share > 0.50 ~
    "FDI-dominated revenue structures typically bring higher capital intensity and technology exposure, but may limit domestic capability spillovers if supplier linkages with local firms are weak.",
  as.character(dominant_owner$Ownership) == "SOE" & dominant_owner$revenue_share > 0.50 ~
    "State-owned enterprise dominance often reflects regulated or capital-intensive activities; productivity performance in such sectors tends to depend heavily on reform of managerial incentives rather than investment volume alone.",
  as.character(dominant_owner$Ownership) == "Domestic private firms" & dominant_owner$revenue_share > 0.50 ~
    "A domestic-private-led revenue structure places firms that face competitive market pressure at the centre of the sector; productivity gains tend to be driven by selection, entry and capability upgrading rather than by state direction.",
  TRUE ~
    "The mixed ownership profile means productivity dynamics are shaped by interactions between firm types with different incentive structures, investment horizons and technology access."
)

interp(glue(
  "Pooled across {base_year}-{snapshot_year}, **{dominant_owner$Ownership}** accounts for the largest share of ",
  "{target_sector} revenue at **{percent(dominant_owner$revenue_share, accuracy = 0.1)}**, {gap_dominant_msg}. ",
  "**{second_owner$Ownership}** is the second-largest contributor at **{percent(second_owner$revenue_share, accuracy = 0.1)}**, ",
  "{gap_second_dir} its all-sector equivalent (**{percent(all_second, accuracy = 0.1)}**). ",
  "{structural_ownership_msg}"
))
```

## 1.4 Firm Entry and Exit Dynamics

<p class="section-desc">

Annual counts of entering, exiting, and staying firms alongside the sector output index (`r base_year` = 100), showing whether firm turnover is accompanied by output growth or decline.

</p>

```{r page1-visual3, fig.cap=paste0('Figure 1.3: Annual firm entry, exit, and incumbent counts with sector output index (', base_year, ' = 100), ', base_year, '–', snapshot_year, '.')}
firm_flow_data <- firm_flow_data %>%
  mutate(
    x_pos               = as.numeric(factor(survey_year)),
    output_index_scaled = (output_index - index_min_plot) * scale_factor_v3 + firm_axis_min
  )

ggplot() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "#6B7280", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_col(
    data = bar_positive,
    aes(x = x_pos, y = `Number of firms`, fill = `Firm flow`),
    width = 0.46, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  geom_col(
    data = bar_exit,
    aes(x = x_pos, y = `Number of firms`, fill = `Firm flow`),
    width = 0.28, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  geom_smooth(
    data = firm_flow_data,
    aes(x = x_pos, y = output_index_scaled, colour = "Output index (2019 = 100)", group = 1),
    method = "loess", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.4, span = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = firm_flow_data,
    aes(x = x_pos, y = output_index_scaled, colour = "Output index (2019 = 100)"),
    size = 3
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = firm_flow_data,
    aes(x = x_pos - 0.16, y = total_active_firms, label = comma(total_active_firms)),
    vjust = -0.5, size = 3.3, fontface = "bold", colour = "#12355B"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = firm_flow_data %>% filter(exit > 0),
    aes(x = x_pos + 0.22, y = -exit, label = comma(exit)),
    vjust = 1.5, size = 3.3, fontface = "bold", colour = "#E76F51"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = firm_flow_data$x_pos, labels = firm_flow_data$survey_year) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name   = "Number of firms",
    labels = comma_format(),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.15, 0.15)),
    sec.axis = sec_axis(
      ~ (. - firm_axis_min) / scale_factor_v3 + index_min_plot,
      name   = "Output index (2019 = 100)",
      labels = number_format(accuracy = 1)
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Staying (incumbents)" = "#1B9E77", "Entry" = "#66A61E", "Exit" = "#E76F51"),
    name = "Firm flow"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("Output index (2019 = 100)" = "#F4A261"),
    name = "Trend line"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = glue("{target_sector}: Firm Entry and Exit Dynamics"),
    subtitle = paste(
      "Bars show incumbents, entrants and exits; numbers on bars show total active firms and exit counts.",
      "\nLeft axis measures number of firms. Right axis measures output index (2019 = 100)."
    ),
    x       = NULL,
    caption = paste(
      "Staying and entry bars are stacked above zero. Exit bars are shown separately below zero.",
      "\nThe orange line (right axis) tracks sector real revenue relative to 2019, where 2019 = 100."
    )
  ) +
  guides(
    fill   = guide_legend(order = 1),
    colour = guide_legend(order = 2, override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.25, size = 3))
  ) +
  theme_sector() +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.box         = "vertical",
    legend.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y.left  = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#12355B"),
    axis.text.y.left   = element_text(colour = "#12355B"),
    axis.title.y.right = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#F4A261"),
    axis.text.y.right  = element_text(colour = "#F4A261")
  )
```

```{r page1-visual3-interpretation, results='asis'}
firm_2023 <- firm_flow_data %>% filter(survey_year == snapshot_year)

entry_exit_msg <- if_else(
  firm_2023$entry > firm_2023$exit,
  "Entry exceeded exit, suggesting positive firm renewal and sector attractiveness.",
  "Exit exceeded entry, indicating restructuring pressure or consolidation within the sector."
)

output_direction <- if_else(
  firm_2023$output_index >= 100,
  glue("expanded to an index of **{round(firm_2023$output_index, 1)}** (2019 = 100)"),
  glue("contracted to an index of **{round(firm_2023$output_index, 1)}** (2019 = 100)")
)

# Renewal-vs-output narrative: correlate firm count with output index across all years
corr_firm_output <- tryCatch(
  cor(firm_flow_data$total_active_firms, firm_flow_data$output_index, use = "complete.obs"),
  error = function(e) NA_real_
)

avg_annual_churn_v3 <- firm_flow_data %>%
  filter(survey_year > base_year) %>%
  summarise(churn = mean(safe_divide(entry + exit, total_active_firms), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(churn)

high_churn_v3 <- !is.na(avg_annual_churn_v3) && avg_annual_churn_v3 >= 0.10
pos_corr_v3   <- !is.na(corr_firm_output) && corr_firm_output > 0.5
neg_corr_v3   <- !is.na(corr_firm_output) && corr_firm_output < -0.5

renewal_narrative <- case_when(
  pos_corr_v3 & high_churn_v3 ~
    "Across the period, firm count and output moved together with high churn, consistent with renewal driving output: new entrants reshaped the productive base while aggregate revenue expanded.",
  pos_corr_v3 & !high_churn_v3 ~
    "Firm count and output moved together against a broadly stable firm population, consistent with incumbent-led growth rather than renewal-driven expansion.",
  neg_corr_v3 ~
    "Firm count and output moved in opposite directions across the period, suggesting restructuring: consolidation among fewer, more productive firms sustained performance while total firm numbers declined.",
  is.na(corr_firm_output) ~
    "The relationship between firm population dynamics and aggregate output could not be assessed with available data.",
  TRUE ~
    "Firm count and output moved largely independently, suggesting the link between firm renewal and aggregate performance was weak across the period."
)

interp(glue(
  "In {snapshot_year}, {target_sector} had **{comma(firm_2023$total_active_firms)}** active firms. ",
  "**{comma(firm_2023$entry)}** firms entered and **{comma(firm_2023$exit)}** exited. {entry_exit_msg} ",
  "Sector output {output_direction}. ",
  "{renewal_narrative}"
))
```

## 1.5 Labour Inclusion and Productivity Performance

<p class="section-desc">

Labour productivity and female labour share benchmarked against all-sector averages, `r base_year`-`r snapshot_year`. Left axis: labour productivity (million VND per worker). Right axis: female labour share.

</p>

```{r page1-visual4-prep, include=FALSE}
v4_all_sector_female <- df_page1 %>%
  group_by(survey_year) %>%
  summarise(
    all_female_labour            = sum(labour_female_headcount_end, na.rm = TRUE),
    all_labour                   = sum(labour_headcount_end, na.rm = TRUE),
    female_labour_share_all_sector = safe_divide(all_female_labour, all_labour),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

v4_all_sector_productivity <- all_sector_year %>%
  group_by(survey_year) %>%
  summarise(average_sector_productivity = mean(labour_productivity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

v4_plot_data <- sector_year %>%
  select(survey_year, female_labour_share, labour_productivity) %>%
  left_join(v4_all_sector_female, by = "survey_year") %>%
  left_join(v4_all_sector_productivity, by = "survey_year")

productivity_sector_label  <- glue("Productivity - {target_sector}")
productivity_average_label <- "Average Productivity - All Sectors"
female_sector_label        <- glue("Female Labour Share - {target_sector}")
female_all_label           <- "Female Labour Share - All Sectors"

v4_bar <- v4_plot_data %>%
  transmute(
    survey_year,
    !!productivity_sector_label  := labour_productivity,
    !!productivity_average_label := average_sector_productivity
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(all_of(productivity_sector_label), all_of(productivity_average_label)),
    names_to = "Productivity benchmark", values_to = "Productivity"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    `Productivity benchmark` = factor(`Productivity benchmark`,
      levels = c(productivity_sector_label, productivity_average_label))
  )

v4_line <- v4_plot_data %>%
  transmute(
    survey_year,
    !!female_sector_label := female_labour_share,
    !!female_all_label    := female_labour_share_all_sector
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(all_of(female_sector_label), all_of(female_all_label)),
    names_to = "Female labour benchmark", values_to = "Female labour share"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    `Female labour benchmark` = factor(`Female labour benchmark`,
      levels = c(female_sector_label, female_all_label))
  )

prod_min      <- 0
prod_max      <- max(v4_bar$Productivity, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.22
female_min    <- min(v4_line$`Female labour share`, na.rm = TRUE)
female_max    <- max(v4_line$`Female labour share`, na.rm = TRUE)
female_padding <- (female_max - female_min) * 0.20
female_min_plot <- max(0, female_min - female_padding)
female_max_plot <- min(1, female_max + female_padding)
scale_factor_v4 <- (prod_max - prod_min) / (female_max_plot - female_min_plot)

v4_line <- v4_line %>%
  mutate(female_share_scaled = (`Female labour share` - female_min_plot) * scale_factor_v4 + prod_min)
```

```{r page1-visual4, fig.cap=paste0('Figure 1.4: Labour productivity (million VND per worker) and female labour share benchmarked against all-sector averages, ', base_year, '–', snapshot_year, '.')}
ggplot() +
  geom_col(
    data = v4_bar,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = Productivity, fill = `Productivity benchmark`),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7), width = 0.58, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.35
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = v4_line,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = female_share_scaled,
        colour = `Female labour benchmark`, group = `Female labour benchmark`),
    linewidth = 1.25
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = v4_line,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = female_share_scaled,
        colour = `Female labour benchmark`, shape = `Female labour benchmark`),
    size = 3
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = v4_bar,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = Productivity,
        label = comma(round(Productivity, 0)),
        group = `Productivity benchmark`),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    vjust = -0.45, size = 3, fontface = "bold", colour = "#12355B"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name   = "Labour productivity (million VND per worker)",
    labels = comma_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(prod_min, prod_max),
    sec.axis = sec_axis(
      ~ (. - prod_min) / scale_factor_v4 + female_min_plot,
      name   = "Female labour share",
      labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = setNames(c("#2A9D8F", "#D9E1E8"), c(productivity_sector_label, productivity_average_label)),
    name = "Productivity benchmark", drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = setNames(c("#E76F51", "#6B7280"), c(female_sector_label, female_all_label)),
    name = "Female labour benchmark", drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = setNames(c(16, 15), c(female_sector_label, female_all_label)),
    name = "Female labour benchmark"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = glue("{target_sector}: Labour Inclusion and Productivity Performance"),
    subtitle = paste(
      glue("Bars compare {target_sector} productivity with average all-sector productivity; lines compare female labour share"),
      "\nLeft axis measures labour productivity. Right axis measures female labour share."
    ),
    x = NULL
  ) +
  guides(
    fill   = guide_legend(order = 1),
    colour = guide_legend(order = 2, override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.3, size = 3)),
    shape  = guide_legend(order = 2)
  ) +
  theme_sector() +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.box         = "vertical",
    legend.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y.left  = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#2A9D8F"),
    axis.text.y.left   = element_text(colour = "#2A9D8F"),
    axis.title.y.right = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#E76F51"),
    axis.text.y.right  = element_text(colour = "#E76F51")
  )
```

```{r page1-visual4-interpretation, results='asis'}
v4_2019      <- v4_plot_data %>% filter(survey_year == base_year)
v4_2023      <- v4_plot_data %>% filter(survey_year == snapshot_year)
prod_gap     <- v4_2023$labour_productivity - v4_2023$average_sector_productivity
prod_gap_pct <- safe_divide(prod_gap, v4_2023$average_sector_productivity)
prod_dir     <- if_else(prod_gap >= 0, "above", "below")
female_gap   <- v4_2023$female_labour_share - v4_2023$female_labour_share_all_sector
female_dir   <- if_else(female_gap >= 0, "higher than", "lower than")
female_gap_pp <- abs(female_gap) * 100

# LP gap trend: base year vs snapshot year
prod_gap_base <- if (nrow(v4_2019) > 0) {
  v4_2019$labour_productivity - v4_2019$average_sector_productivity
} else { NA_real_ }

prod_trend_msg <- case_when(
  is.na(prod_gap_base) | is.na(prod_gap) ~ "",
  prod_gap > 0 & prod_gap_base <= 0 ~
    "The sector has moved from below to above the economy average in productivity since the base year, recovering a deficit recorded in the base year.",
  prod_gap > 0 & prod_gap_base > 0 & prod_gap > prod_gap_base ~
    "The sector's productivity advantage over the economy average has strengthened since the base year.",
  prod_gap > 0 & prod_gap <= prod_gap_base ~
    "The sector retains a productivity advantage but it has narrowed relative to the base year.",
  prod_gap < 0 & prod_gap > prod_gap_base ~
    "The sector remains below the economy average but has been closing the gap, indicating catch-up dynamics.",
  prod_gap < 0 & prod_gap <= prod_gap_base ~
    "The sector's productivity shortfall relative to the economy average has deepened since the base year.",
  TRUE ~ ""
)

# 4-branch inclusion quadrant
inclusion_msg <- case_when(
  prod_gap >= 0 & female_gap >= 0 ~
    "Above-average productivity alongside above-average female labour share suggests the sector combines economic performance with broad workforce participation , a favourable combination from both efficiency and inclusion perspectives.",
  prod_gap >= 0 & female_gap < 0 ~
    "Above-average productivity but below-average female labour share may reflect capital- or skill-intensive activity with limited workforce inclusion. Policies that widen access without diluting capability could address both dimensions.",
  prod_gap < 0 & female_gap >= 0 ~
    "Below-average productivity despite above-average female labour inclusion suggests employment concentration in lower-productivity activities; capability upgrading is the more binding lever than further workforce expansion.",
  TRUE ~
    "Below-average performance on both productivity and female inclusion indicates a sector where capability investment and active workforce participation policy are both warranted."
)

interp(glue(
  "In {snapshot_year}, {target_sector} labour productivity was **{comma(round(v4_2023$labour_productivity, 1))} million VND per worker**, ",
  "{prod_dir} the all-sector average by **{percent(abs(prod_gap_pct), accuracy = 0.1)}** ",
  "(**{comma(round(v4_2023$average_sector_productivity, 1))} million VND** all-sector). ",
  "{prod_trend_msg} ",
  "Female labour share stood at **{percent(v4_2023$female_labour_share, accuracy = 0.1)}**, ",
  "{female_dir} the all-sector benchmark (**{percent(v4_2023$female_labour_share_all_sector, accuracy = 0.1)}**) ",
  "by **{round(female_gap_pp, 1)} percentage points**. {inclusion_msg}"
))
```

::: part-divider
<span>Part II: Technology Adoption and Innovation Dynamics</span>
:::

# 2. Technology Adoption and Innovation Dynamics

## 2.1 KPI Snapshot

<p class="section-desc">

Key investment and innovation indicators for `r snapshot_year` vs `r base_year`, covering total investment, innovation expenditure, firm participation rates, and labour productivity.

</p>

```{r page2-kpi, results='asis'}
kpi_base_2 <- sector_year %>%
  filter(survey_year == base_year) %>%
  summarise(
    total_investment_base     = sum(total_investment, na.rm = TRUE),
    innovation_investment_base = sum(innovation_investment, na.rm = TRUE),
    labour_productivity_base  = safe_divide(sum(revenue, na.rm = TRUE), sum(labour, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

kpi_snapshot_raw_2 <- sector_year %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
  summarise(
    total_investment        = sum(total_investment, na.rm = TRUE),
    innovation_investment   = sum(innovation_investment, na.rm = TRUE),
    firms                   = sum(firms, na.rm = TRUE),
    firms_with_any_investment = sum(firms_with_any_selected_investment, na.rm = TRUE),
    labour_productivity     = safe_divide(sum(revenue, na.rm = TRUE), sum(labour, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  bind_cols(kpi_base_2) %>%
  mutate(
    total_investment_growth     = safe_divide(total_investment, total_investment_base) - 1,
    innovation_investment_growth = safe_divide(innovation_investment, innovation_investment_base) - 1,
    labour_productivity_growth  = safe_divide(labour_productivity, labour_productivity_base) - 1,
    any_investment_share        = safe_divide(firms_with_any_investment, firms)
  )

kpi_snapshot_2 <- tibble(
  KPI = c("Total investment", "Firms with any investment", "Labour productivity", "Total innovation investment"),
  Value = c(
    paste0(comma(round(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$total_investment / 1e6, 2)), " trillion VND (", format_change(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$total_investment_growth), " vs 2019)"),
    paste0(percent(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$any_investment_share, accuracy = 0.1), " of firms (", comma(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$firms_with_any_investment), " of ", comma(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$firms), " firms)"),
    paste0(comma(round(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$labour_productivity, 1)), " mn VND/worker (", format_change(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$labour_productivity_growth), " vs 2019)"),
    paste0(comma(round(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$innovation_investment / 1e6, 2)), " trillion VND (", format_change(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$innovation_investment_growth), " vs 2019)")
  )
)

kpi_snapshot_2 %>%
  kable(
    format = "html", escape = FALSE,
    caption = glue("{target_sector}: Page 2 KPI Snapshot, {snapshot_year}")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = TRUE,
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    font_size = 14
  ) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE, color = "#12355B", width = "35%") %>%
  column_spec(2, color = "#1F2933", width = "65%")
```

```{r page2-kpi-interpretation, results='asis'}
innov_share_of_total <- safe_divide(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$innovation_investment, kpi_snapshot_raw_2$total_investment)

# Benchmark innovation share against all-sector average in snapshot year
all_innov_share_snap <- df_final %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
  summarise(
    all_innov = sum(innovation_investment_proxy, na.rm = TRUE),
    all_total = sum(total_investment, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  summarise(share = safe_divide(all_innov, all_total)) %>%
  pull(share)

innov_benchmark_msg <- case_when(
  is.na(innov_share_of_total) | is.na(all_innov_share_snap) ~
    "The all-sector innovation share benchmark is unavailable.",
  innov_share_of_total > all_innov_share_snap + 0.02 ~
    glue("This is above the all-sector average of **{percent(all_innov_share_snap, accuracy = 0.1)}**, indicating above-average emphasis on capability-led investment relative to the broader economy."),
  innov_share_of_total < all_innov_share_snap - 0.02 ~
    glue("This is below the all-sector average of **{percent(all_innov_share_snap, accuracy = 0.1)}**, suggesting the sector remains more reliant on physical capital than the broader economy."),
  TRUE ~
    glue("This is broadly in line with the all-sector average of **{percent(all_innov_share_snap, accuracy = 0.1)}**.")
)

interp(glue(
  "In {snapshot_year}, total investment in {target_sector} reached ",
  "**{comma(round(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$total_investment / 1e6, 2))} trillion VND** ",
  "({format_change(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$total_investment_growth)} vs {base_year}). ",
  "**{percent(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$any_investment_share, accuracy = 0.1)}** of firms ",
  "(**{comma(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$firms_with_any_investment)}** of **{comma(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$firms)}**) ",
  "undertook at least one form of selected investment. ",
  "Innovation investment reached **{comma(round(kpi_snapshot_raw_2$innovation_investment / 1e6, 2))} trillion VND**, ",
  "representing **{percent(innov_share_of_total, accuracy = 0.1)}** of total investment. ",
  "{innov_benchmark_msg}"
))
```

## 2.2 Investment Scale and Adoption Diffusion

<p class="section-desc">

Total investment volume and the share of firms investing in equipment and innovation, `r base_year`–`r snapshot_year`. Left axis: investment value (trillion VND). Right axis: share of firms investing.

</p>

```{r page2-visual1-prep, include=FALSE}
investment_levels <- c("Equipment investment", "Innovation Investment")

v1_dual <- sector_year %>%
  select(survey_year,
    `Equipment investment`  = equipment_investment,
    `Innovation Investment` = innovation_investment
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -survey_year, names_to = "Investment type", values_to = "Investment value") %>%
  mutate(
    `Investment type`   = factor(`Investment type`, levels = investment_levels),
    investment_trillion = `Investment value` / 1e6
  )

v1_rate_dual <- sector_year %>%
  select(survey_year,
    `Equipment investment`  = equipment_adoption_rate,
    `Innovation Investment` = innovation_adoption_rate
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -survey_year, names_to = "Investment type", values_to = "Adoption rate") %>%
  mutate(`Investment type` = factor(`Investment type`, levels = investment_levels))

max_investment <- max(v1_dual$investment_trillion, na.rm = TRUE)
max_rate       <- max(v1_rate_dual$`Adoption rate`, na.rm = TRUE)
scale_factor   <- ifelse(max_rate > 0, max_investment / max_rate, 1)
```

```{r page2-visual1, fig.cap=paste0('Figure 2.1: Total equipment and innovation investment value (trillion VND) and share of firms investing, ', base_year, '–', snapshot_year, '.')}
ggplot() +
  geom_col(
    data = v1_dual,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = investment_trillion, fill = `Investment type`),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.72), width = 0.62, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = v1_rate_dual,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = `Adoption rate` * scale_factor,
        colour = `Investment type`, group = `Investment type`),
    linewidth = 0.85, show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = v1_rate_dual,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = `Adoption rate` * scale_factor, colour = `Investment type`),
    size = 2.5, show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = v1_dual,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = investment_trillion,
        label = comma(round(investment_trillion, 2)),
        group = `Investment type`),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.72),
    vjust = -0.45, size = 3, fontface = "bold", colour = "#12355B"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name   = "Total investment value (trillion VND)",
    labels = comma_format(accuracy = 0.01),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)),
    sec.axis = sec_axis(
      ~ . / scale_factor,
      name   = "Share of firms investing",
      labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Equipment investment" = "#2A9D8F", "Innovation Investment" = "#F4A261"),
    name = "Investment value", drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("Equipment investment" = "#12355B", "Innovation Investment" = "#E76F51"),
    name = "Adoption rate", drop = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = glue("{target_sector}: Investment Scale and Adoption Diffusion"),
    subtitle = paste(
      "Bars show total investment value; lines show share of firms investing",
      "\nLeft axis measures total investment value (trillion VND). Right axis measures the share of firms investing."
    ),
    x = NULL
  ) +
  guides(
    fill   = guide_legend(title = "Investment value", order = 1),
    colour = guide_legend(title = "Adoption rate", order = 2, override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.1, size = 2.8))
  ) +
  theme_sector() +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.box         = "vertical",
    legend.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y.left  = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#12355B"),
    axis.text.y.left   = element_text(colour = "#12355B"),
    axis.title.y.right = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#E76F51"),
    axis.text.y.right  = element_text(colour = "#E76F51")
  )
```

```{r page2-visual1-interpretation, results='asis'}
equip_rate_2023 <- v1_rate_dual %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year, `Investment type` == "Equipment investment") %>%
  pull(`Adoption rate`)
innov_rate_2023 <- v1_rate_dual %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year, `Investment type` == "Innovation Investment") %>%
  pull(`Adoption rate`)
equip_val_2023  <- v1_dual %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year, `Investment type` == "Equipment investment") %>%
  pull(investment_trillion)
innov_val_2023  <- v1_dual %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year, `Investment type` == "Innovation Investment") %>%
  pull(investment_trillion)

adoption_gap_pp_v1 <- abs(equip_rate_2023 - innov_rate_2023) * 100

adoption_gap_msg <- case_when(
  adoption_gap_pp_v1 <= 5 ~
    "Equipment and innovation adoption rates are closely matched, suggesting firms treat the two investment types as complementary rather than substitutes.",
  equip_rate_2023 > innov_rate_2023 + 0.05 ~
    glue("Equipment adoption is materially higher than innovation adoption ({percent(equip_rate_2023, accuracy = 0.1)} vs {percent(innov_rate_2023, accuracy = 0.1)}), consistent with a sector still in an earlier upgrading phase where hardware investment leads capability development."),
  innov_rate_2023 > equip_rate_2023 + 0.05 ~
    glue("Innovation adoption is materially higher than equipment adoption ({percent(innov_rate_2023, accuracy = 0.1)} vs {percent(equip_rate_2023, accuracy = 0.1)}), suggesting firms are emphasising intangible capability ahead of physical hardware , an advanced adoption profile."),
  TRUE ~ ""
)

diffusion_level_msg <- case_when(
  max(equip_rate_2023, innov_rate_2023) < 0.10 ~
    "Overall adoption remains very limited, with the large majority of firms not investing in either category.",
  max(equip_rate_2023, innov_rate_2023) < 0.20 ~
    "Adoption is still in early diffusion across both categories, with most firms not yet participating in either type of investment.",
  max(equip_rate_2023, innov_rate_2023) < 0.40 ~
    "Adoption has reached moderate breadth in at least one category, suggesting technology investment is beginning to move beyond early adopters.",
  TRUE ~
    "Adoption is relatively broad-based across at least one investment type, indicating technology investment has diffused well beyond a narrow group of early adopters."
)

interp(glue(
  "In {snapshot_year}, equipment investment was adopted by **{percent(equip_rate_2023, accuracy = 0.1)}** of firms ",
  "(**{comma(round(equip_val_2023, 2))} trillion VND** total), while innovation investment was adopted by ",
  "**{percent(innov_rate_2023, accuracy = 0.1)}** of firms (**{comma(round(innov_val_2023, 2))} trillion VND** total). ",
  "{adoption_gap_msg} ",
  "{diffusion_level_msg}"
))
```

## 2.3 Investment Allocation Mix and Total Investment

<p class="section-desc">

Stacked shares of investment categories (machinery, innovation, construction, repairs) and total investment volume, `r base_year`–`r snapshot_year`. Left axis: allocation share. Right axis: total investment (trillion VND).

</p>

```{r page2-visual2-prep, include=FALSE}
v2_plot_data <- sector_df %>%
  filter(has_equipment_investment == 1 | has_innovation_investment == 1 | total_investment > 0) %>%
  group_by(survey_year) %>%
  summarise(
    `Machinery and equipment`      = sum(investment_machinery_equipment, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Innovation Investment`        = sum(innovation_investment_proxy, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Construction and installation` = sum(investment_construction_installation, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Other construction`           = sum(investment_construction_other, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Major repairs`                = sum(investment_major_repairs, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

v2_mix <- v2_plot_data %>%
  pivot_longer(-survey_year, names_to = "Investment category", values_to = "Value") %>%
  group_by(survey_year) %>%
  mutate(
    total_value = sum(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    share       = if_else(total_value > 0, Value / total_value, NA_real_)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(!is.na(share))

v2_total <- v2_plot_data %>%
  mutate(
    total_investment_selected = `Machinery and equipment` + `Innovation Investment` +
      `Construction and installation` + `Other construction` + `Major repairs`,
    total_investment_trillion = total_investment_selected / 1e6
  ) %>%
  select(survey_year, total_investment_trillion)

scale_factor_v2 <- max(v2_total$total_investment_trillion, na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r page2-visual2, fig.cap=paste0('Figure 2.2: Investment allocation shares by category and total selected investment volume (trillion VND), ', base_year, '–', snapshot_year, '.')}
ggplot() +
  geom_col(
    data = v2_mix,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = share, fill = `Investment category`),
    width = 0.72, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.28
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = v2_total,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = total_investment_trillion / scale_factor_v2,
        group = 1, colour = "Total investment"),
    linewidth = 1.3
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = v2_total,
    aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = total_investment_trillion / scale_factor_v2,
        colour = "Total investment"),
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name   = "Investment allocation share",
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    sec.axis = sec_axis(
      ~ . * scale_factor_v2,
      name   = "Total selected investment (trillion VND)",
      labels = comma_format(accuracy = 0.01)
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Machinery and equipment"       = "#2A9D8F",
      "Innovation Investment"         = "#F4A261",
      "Construction and installation" = "#8ECAE6",
      "Other construction"            = "#B8C0CC",
      "Major repairs"                 = "#E9C46A"
    ),
    name = "Investment category", drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Total investment" = "#12355B"), name = "Total investment line") +
  labs(
    title    = glue("{target_sector}: Investment Allocation Mix and Total Investment"),
    subtitle = paste(
      "Bars show allocation shares; line shows total selected investment value",
      "\nLeft axis measures investment allocation share. Right axis measures total selected investment value (trillion VND)."
    ),
    x = NULL
  ) +
  guides(
    fill   = guide_legend(order = 1, override.aes = list(alpha = 1)),
    colour = guide_legend(order = 2, override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.3, size = 3))
  ) +
  theme_sector() +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.box         = "vertical",
    legend.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 10.5, colour = "#6B7280", lineheight = 1.25),
    axis.title.y.left  = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#2A9D8F"),
    axis.text.y.left   = element_text(colour = "#2A9D8F"),
    axis.title.y.right = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#12355B"),
    axis.text.y.right  = element_text(colour = "#12355B")
  )
```

```{r page2-visual2-interpretation, results='asis'}
dominant_investment  <- v2_mix %>% filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>% arrange(desc(share)) %>% slice(1)
total_selected_2023  <- v2_total %>% filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>% pull(total_investment_trillion)

innov_share_base     <- v2_mix %>%
  filter(survey_year == base_year, `Investment category` == "Innovation Investment") %>%
  pull(share)
innov_share_snap     <- v2_mix %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year, `Investment category` == "Innovation Investment") %>%
  pull(share)
innov_share_base     <- if (length(innov_share_base) > 0) innov_share_base else NA_real_
innov_share_snap     <- if (length(innov_share_snap) > 0) innov_share_snap else NA_real_
innov_trend_pp       <- (innov_share_snap - innov_share_base) * 100
innov_trend_dir      <- if_else(!is.na(innov_trend_pp) && innov_trend_pp >= 0, "rose", "fell")

investment_mix_msg <- case_when(
  dominant_investment$`Investment category` == "Machinery and equipment" ~
    "This indicates a stronger focus on hard-technology adoption and physical capital upgrading.",
  dominant_investment$`Investment category` == "Innovation Investment" ~
    "This indicates a stronger emphasis on innovation capability, R&D, and soft-technology investment.",
  TRUE ~
    "Investment is directed primarily toward infrastructure, construction, or maintenance activities."
)

innov_trend_sentence <- if_else(
  !is.na(innov_trend_pp),
  glue(
    "The innovation investment share {innov_trend_dir} from **{percent(innov_share_base, accuracy = 0.1)}** ",
    "in {base_year} to **{percent(innov_share_snap, accuracy = 0.1)}** in {snapshot_year} ",
    "({if_else(innov_trend_pp >= 0, '+', '')}**{round(innov_trend_pp, 1)} percentage points**), ",
    "indicating {if_else(innov_trend_pp >= 0, 'a growing', 'a shrinking')} role for innovation ",
    "relative to physical capital."
  ),
  ""
)

interp(glue(
  "In {snapshot_year}, total selected investment reached **{comma(round(total_selected_2023, 2))} trillion VND**. ",
  "The largest allocation was {dominant_investment$`Investment category`} ",
  "at **{percent(dominant_investment$share, accuracy = 0.1)}**. {investment_mix_msg} ",
  "{innov_trend_sentence}"
))
```

## 2.4 Frontier vs Laggard Gap over Time

<p class="section-desc">

Productivity and investment intensity ratios between the top 3% of firms (frontier) and the remaining 97% (laggards), showing whether the performance gap is widening or narrowing.

</p>

```{r page2-visual3-prep, include=FALSE}
frontier_year_base <- sector_df %>%
  mutate(firm_productivity = safe_divide(total_net_revenue, labour_headcount_end)) %>%
  filter(labour_headcount_end > 0, firm_productivity > 0) %>%
  group_by(survey_year) %>%
  mutate(
    productivity_rank = percent_rank(firm_productivity),
    frontier_group    = if_else(productivity_rank >= 0.97, "Frontier: top 3%", "Laggard: remaining 97%")
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

frontier_year_metrics <- frontier_year_base %>%
  group_by(survey_year, frontier_group) %>%
  summarise(
    `Labour productivity`              = median(firm_productivity, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Equipment investment per worker`  = safe_divide(sum(investment_machinery_equipment, na.rm = TRUE), sum(labour_headcount_end, na.rm = TRUE)),
    `Innovation investment per worker` = safe_divide(sum(innovation_investment_proxy, na.rm = TRUE), sum(labour_headcount_end, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(`Labour productivity`, `Equipment investment per worker`, `Innovation investment per worker`),
    names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = frontier_group, values_from = Value) %>%
  mutate(
    Ratio = safe_divide(`Frontier: top 3%`, `Laggard: remaining 97%`),
    Label = paste0(round(Ratio, 1), "x")
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(Ratio)) %>%
  mutate(
    Metric = factor(Metric, levels = c(
      "Labour productivity",
      "Equipment investment per worker",
      "Innovation investment per worker"
    ))
  )
```

```{r page2-visual3, fig.cap=paste0('Figure 2.3: Frontier-to-laggard ratio for labour productivity and investment intensity (top 3% vs remaining 97% of firms), ', base_year, '–', snapshot_year, '.')}
ggplot(frontier_year_metrics, aes(x = survey_year, y = Ratio, colour = Metric, group = Metric)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, colour = "#B8C0CC", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.4) +
  geom_point(size = 3.8, stroke = 0.9, shape = 21, fill = "white") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = Label), vjust = -0.9, size = 3.4, fontface = "bold",
    colour = "#12355B", show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(frontier_year_metrics$survey_year))) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = number_format(accuracy = 0.1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.25))
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
      "Labour productivity"              = "#E63946",
      "Equipment investment per worker"  = "#2A9D8F",
      "Innovation investment per worker" = "#F4A261"
    ),
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(title = "Metric", override.aes = list(size = 4, linewidth = 1.5))) +
  labs(
    title    = glue("{target_sector}: Frontier vs Laggard Gap over Time"),
    subtitle = "Top 3% firms vs remaining 97%: multiplier of productivity and investment intensity",
    x       = NULL,
    y       = "Frontier / laggard multiplier",
    colour  = "Metric",
    caption = "Ratio > 1 means frontier firms have higher values than laggard firms."
  ) +
  theme_sector() +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.title     = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 10)
  )
```

```{r page2-visual3-interpretation, results='asis'}
frontier_2023 <- frontier_year_metrics %>% filter(survey_year == snapshot_year)
frontier_base <- frontier_year_metrics %>% filter(survey_year == base_year)

prod_ratio      <- frontier_2023 %>% filter(Metric == "Labour productivity") %>% pull(Ratio)
equip_ratio     <- frontier_2023 %>% filter(Metric == "Equipment investment per worker") %>% pull(Ratio)
innov_ratio     <- frontier_2023 %>% filter(Metric == "Innovation investment per worker") %>% pull(Ratio)
prod_ratio_base <- frontier_base %>% filter(Metric == "Labour productivity") %>% pull(Ratio)

prod_ratio_base_val <- if (length(prod_ratio_base) > 0 && !is.na(prod_ratio_base[1])) prod_ratio_base[1] else NA_real_
prod_ratio_val      <- if (length(prod_ratio) > 0 && !is.na(prod_ratio[1])) prod_ratio[1] else NA_real_

gap_trend <- if_else(
  !is.na(prod_ratio_base_val) && !is.na(prod_ratio_val),
  if_else(prod_ratio_val > prod_ratio_base_val, "widened", "narrowed"),
  "could not be trended with available data"
)

trend_detail <- if_else(
  !is.na(prod_ratio_base_val) && !is.na(prod_ratio_val),
  glue("The productivity gap has {gap_trend} since {base_year} (**{round(prod_ratio_base_val, 1)}x** then vs **{round(prod_ratio_val, 1)}x** now). "),
  ""
)

gap_msg <- if_else(
  !is.na(prod_ratio_val) && prod_ratio_val > 10,
  "The large gap points to substantial unevenness, with a small frontier cohort capturing a disproportionate share of sector productivity.",
  "The productivity gap is present but less extreme, suggesting some degree of capability diffusion across the sector."
)

equip_ratio_val <- if (length(equip_ratio) > 0 && !is.na(equip_ratio[1])) equip_ratio[1] else NA_real_
innov_ratio_val <- if (length(innov_ratio) > 0 && !is.na(innov_ratio[1])) innov_ratio[1] else NA_real_

binding_gap_msg <- case_when(
  is.na(equip_ratio_val) | is.na(innov_ratio_val) ~
    "The two diffusion gaps could not be compared with available data.",
  equip_ratio_val > innov_ratio_val + 0.5 ~
    "The larger gap is in equipment intensity, pointing to capital and financing constraints as the more binding barrier when laggard firms attempt to upgrade machinery.",
  innov_ratio_val > equip_ratio_val + 0.5 ~
    "The larger gap is in innovation intensity, pointing to skills, organisational capability and digital readiness as the more binding constraint on laggard firm catch-up.",
  TRUE ~
    "The equipment-intensity and innovation-intensity gaps are of similar magnitude, indicating that capital and capability constraints likely operate together rather than one dominating the other."
)

interp(glue(
  "In {snapshot_year}, frontier firms (top 3%) in {target_sector} were **{round(prod_ratio_val, 1)}x** more productive ",
  "than laggard firms (remaining 97%). Their equipment and innovation investment intensity were ",
  "**{round(equip_ratio_val, 1)}x** and **{round(innov_ratio_val, 1)}x** higher, respectively. ",
  "{trend_detail}{gap_msg} ",
  "{binding_gap_msg}"
))
```

## 2.5 Equipment Investment Cohort Panel

<p class="section-desc">

Equipment investment persistence among balanced-panel firms (observed in all `r length(unique(sector_df$survey_year))` survey years), grouped by the year of first equipment investment.

</p>

```{r page2-visual4-prep, include=FALSE}
# Balanced panel definition: firms observed in every survey year for this sector.
# Restricting to balanced-panel firms ensures that investment persistence is
# measured on the same set of firms across all years, avoiding compositional
# changes caused by entry or exit. Firms that appear in fewer than all years
# are excluded because their absence in some years cannot be distinguished from
# zero investment versus non-response or exit.
all_years <- sort(unique(sector_df$survey_year))
n_years   <- length(all_years)

balanced_firms <- sector_df %>%
  distinct(tax_id, survey_year) %>%
  count(tax_id, name = "n_years_observed") %>%
  filter(n_years_observed == n_years) %>%
  pull(tax_id)

sector_df_balanced <- sector_df %>% filter(tax_id %in% balanced_firms)

firm_years_balanced <- sector_df_balanced %>%
  select(tax_id, survey_year, has_equipment_investment) %>%
  distinct()

first_invest_year_balanced <- firm_years_balanced %>%
  filter(has_equipment_investment == 1) %>%
  group_by(tax_id) %>%
  summarise(first_equipment_year = min(survey_year), .groups = "drop")

min_year <- min(firm_years_balanced$survey_year, na.rm = TRUE)

cohort_df_balanced <- firm_years_balanced %>%
  left_join(first_invest_year_balanced, by = "tax_id") %>%
  mutate(cohort = case_when(
    is.na(first_equipment_year)        ~ "Never investor",
    first_equipment_year <= min_year   ~ paste0("Already investor in ", min_year),
    TRUE                               ~ paste0("First invested in ", first_equipment_year)
  ))

cohort_order_balanced <- cohort_df_balanced %>%
  distinct(cohort, first_equipment_year) %>%
  mutate(order_value = case_when(
    cohort == "Never investor"          ~ 9999,
    str_detect(cohort, "Already investor") ~ 0,
    TRUE                                ~ first_equipment_year
  )) %>%
  arrange(desc(order_value)) %>%
  pull(cohort)

cohort_plot_data_balanced <- cohort_df_balanced %>%
  group_by(cohort, survey_year) %>%
  summarise(
    n_observed  = n_distinct(tax_id),
    n_investing = n_distinct(tax_id[has_equipment_investment == 1]),
    share_investing = safe_divide(n_investing, n_observed),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    cohort = factor(cohort, levels = unique(cohort_order_balanced)),
    label  = paste0(percent(share_investing, accuracy = 0.1), "\n", comma(n_investing), " of ", comma(n_observed))
  )
```

```{r page2-visual4, fig.cap=paste0('Figure 2.4: Equipment investment persistence by cohort among balanced-panel firms observed in all ', n_years, ' survey years.')}
ggplot(cohort_plot_data_balanced, aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = cohort, fill = share_investing)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), size = 3.15, fontface = "bold", colour = "#1F2933", lineheight = 0.9) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#FFFBF0", high = "#4DB6AC",
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title   = glue("{target_sector}: Equipment Investment Cohort Panel"),
    subtitle = glue("Balanced panel only: firms observed in all {n_years} years"),
    x       = "Survey year",
    y       = "Equipment investment cohort",
    fill    = "Share investing",
    caption = glue(
      "Balanced-panel firms only. N = {comma(length(balanced_firms))}. ",
      "Cell labels show percentage and number of investing firms among firms in each cohort-year."
    )
  ) +
  theme_sector() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
```

```{r page2-visual4-interpretation, results='asis'}
n_balanced    <- length(balanced_firms)
n_total_firms <- n_distinct(sector_df$tax_id)
balanced_share <- safe_divide(n_balanced, n_total_firms)

never_count <- cohort_plot_data_balanced %>%
  filter(str_detect(as.character(cohort), "Never investor"), survey_year == base_year) %>%
  pull(n_observed)
never_share <- safe_divide(
  if (length(never_count) > 0) sum(never_count) else 0,
  n_balanced
)

early_rate_raw <- cohort_plot_data_balanced %>%
  filter(str_detect(as.character(cohort), "Already investor"), survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
  pull(share_investing)
early_rate <- if (length(early_rate_raw) > 0 && !is.na(early_rate_raw[1])) early_rate_raw[1] else NA_real_

early_msg <- if_else(
  !is.na(early_rate),
  glue(
    "Among firms already investing in {base_year}, **{percent(early_rate, accuracy = 0.1)}** continued ",
