    "investing in {snapshot_year}, indicating {if_else(early_rate > 0.5, 'strong', 'moderate')} ",
    "investment persistence."
  ),
  glue(
    "No 'already investor in {base_year}' cohort was observed, suggesting most equipment adoption ",
    "in this sector began after {base_year}."
  )
)

# Representativeness signal
panel_rep_msg <- case_when(
  balanced_share >= 0.60 ~
    "The balanced panel covers the majority of unique firms, so cohort persistence rates are broadly representative of the sector.",
  balanced_share >= 0.35 ~
    "The balanced panel covers a minority of unique firms; results reflect long-tenure incumbents and may understate adoption volatility among transient entrants and exits.",
  TRUE ~
    "Only a small share of all unique firms appear in every survey year; the cohort analysis should be read as descriptive of long-tenure incumbents only."
)

# Latest-cohort adoption trajectory vs early cohort
latest_cohort_rate_raw <- cohort_plot_data_balanced %>%
  filter(str_detect(as.character(cohort), as.character(snapshot_year)), survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
  pull(share_investing)
latest_cohort_rate <- if (length(latest_cohort_rate_raw) > 0 && !is.na(latest_cohort_rate_raw[1])) latest_cohort_rate_raw[1] else NA_real_

cohort_trajectory_msg <- if (!is.na(latest_cohort_rate) && !is.na(early_rate)) {
  if_else(
    latest_cohort_rate >= early_rate - 0.10,
    "New-entry cohorts maintain adoption rates comparable to earlier cohorts, suggesting the equipment investment market in this sector is not saturating.",
    "New-entry cohorts show lower adoption rates than earlier cohorts, consistent with either higher entry barriers for newer firms or later entrants being structurally less capital-intensive."
  )
} else { "" }

interp(glue(
  "The balanced panel covers **{comma(n_balanced)}** firms observed in all {n_years} survey years ",
  "(**{percent(balanced_share, accuracy = 0.1)}** of all unique firms in this sector). ",
  "{panel_rep_msg} ",
  "**{percent(never_share, accuracy = 0.1)}** of balanced-panel firms never invested in equipment ",
  "across the full period. {early_msg} ",
  "{cohort_trajectory_msg}"
))
```

## 2.6 Provincial Technology Adoption Maps

<p class="section-desc">

Equipment and innovation adoption rates mapped across Vietnam's 34 consolidated provinces for `r base_year` and `r snapshot_year`, showing where technology investment is geographically concentrated.

</p>

```{r page2-visual5-prep, include=FALSE}
target_years <- c(2019, 2023)

province_lookup_old <- tibble(
  province_code    = c(1,2,4,6,8,10,11,12,14,15,17,19,20,22,24,25,26,27,30,31,33,34,35,36,37,38,40,42,44,45,46,48,49,51,52,54,56,58,60,62,64,66,67,68,70,72,74,75,77,79,80,82,83,84,86,87,89,91,92,93,94,95,96),
  old_province_name = c("Ha Noi","Ha Giang","Cao Bang","Bac Kan","Tuyen Quang","Lao Cai","Dien Bien","Lai Chau","Son La","Yen Bai","Hoa Binh","Thai Nguyen","Lang Son","Quang Ninh","Bac Giang","Phu Tho","Vinh Phuc","Bac Ninh","Hai Duong","Hai Phong","Hung Yen","Thai Binh","Ha Nam","Nam Dinh","Ninh Binh","Thanh Hoa","Nghe An","Ha Tinh","Quang Binh","Quang Tri","Thua Thien Hue","Da Nang","Quang Nam","Quang Ngai","Binh Dinh","Phu Yen","Khanh Hoa","Ninh Thuan","Binh Thuan","Kon Tum","Gia Lai","Dak Lak","Dak Nong","Lam Dong","Binh Phuoc","Tay Ninh","Binh Duong","Dong Nai","Ba Ria - Vung Tau","Ho Chi Minh City","Long An","Tien Giang","Ben Tre","Tra Vinh","Vinh Long","Dong Thap","An Giang","Kien Giang","Can Tho","Hau Giang","Soc Trang","Bac Lieu","Ca Mau")
) %>% mutate(old_province_key = clean_name(old_province_name))

province_34_mapping <- province_lookup_old %>%
  mutate(
    new_province_name = case_when(
      old_province_name == "Ha Noi"                                            ~ "Ha Noi",
      old_province_name %in% c("Tuyen Quang", "Ha Giang")                     ~ "Tuyen Quang",
      old_province_name %in% c("Lao Cai", "Yen Bai")                          ~ "Lao Cai",
      old_province_name %in% c("Thai Nguyen", "Bac Kan")                      ~ "Thai Nguyen",
      old_province_name %in% c("Phu Tho", "Vinh Phuc", "Hoa Binh")           ~ "Phu Tho",
      old_province_name %in% c("Bac Ninh", "Bac Giang")                       ~ "Bac Ninh",
      old_province_name %in% c("Hung Yen", "Thai Binh")                       ~ "Hung Yen",
      old_province_name %in% c("Hai Phong", "Hai Duong")                      ~ "Hai Phong",
      old_province_name %in% c("Ninh Binh", "Nam Dinh", "Ha Nam")             ~ "Ninh Binh",
      old_province_name == "Thua Thien Hue"                                   ~ "Hue",
      old_province_name %in% c("Quang Binh", "Quang Tri")                     ~ "Quang Tri",
      old_province_name %in% c("Da Nang", "Quang Nam")                        ~ "Da Nang",
      old_province_name %in% c("Quang Ngai", "Kon Tum")                       ~ "Quang Ngai",
      old_province_name %in% c("Gia Lai", "Binh Dinh")                        ~ "Gia Lai",
      old_province_name %in% c("Khanh Hoa", "Ninh Thuan")                     ~ "Khanh Hoa",
      old_province_name %in% c("Lam Dong", "Binh Thuan", "Dak Nong")          ~ "Lam Dong",
      old_province_name %in% c("Dak Lak", "Phu Yen")                          ~ "Dak Lak",
      old_province_name %in% c("Ho Chi Minh City", "Binh Duong", "Ba Ria - Vung Tau") ~ "Ho Chi Minh City",
      old_province_name %in% c("Dong Nai", "Binh Phuoc")                      ~ "Dong Nai",
      old_province_name %in% c("Tay Ninh", "Long An")                         ~ "Tay Ninh",
      old_province_name %in% c("Can Tho", "Soc Trang", "Hau Giang")           ~ "Can Tho",
      old_province_name %in% c("Vinh Long", "Ben Tre", "Tra Vinh")            ~ "Vinh Long",
      old_province_name %in% c("Dong Thap", "Tien Giang")                     ~ "Dong Thap",
      old_province_name %in% c("Ca Mau", "Bac Lieu")                          ~ "Ca Mau",
      old_province_name %in% c("An Giang", "Kien Giang")                      ~ "An Giang",
      TRUE                                                                     ~ old_province_name
    ),
    new_province_key = clean_name(new_province_name)
  )

province_adoption_34 <- sector_df %>%
  filter(survey_year %in% target_years, !is.na(province_code), !is.na(survey_year)) %>%
  left_join(
    province_34_mapping %>% select(province_code, old_province_name, new_province_name, new_province_key),
    by = "province_code"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(new_province_name)) %>%
  group_by(new_province_name, new_province_key, survey_year) %>%
  summarise(
    total_firms            = n_distinct(tax_id),
    firms_with_equipment   = n_distinct(tax_id[has_equipment_investment == 1]),
    firms_with_innovation  = n_distinct(tax_id[has_innovation_investment == 1]),
    equipment_adoption_rate  = safe_divide(firms_with_equipment, total_firms),
    innovation_adoption_rate = safe_divide(firms_with_innovation, total_firms),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

vn_map_raw <- geodata::gadm(country = "VNM", level = 1, path = tempdir())

vn_map_old <- st_as_sf(vn_map_raw) %>%
  st_make_valid() %>%
  mutate(
    old_province_key = clean_name(NAME_1),
    old_province_key = case_when(
      old_province_key == "hochiminh" ~ "hochiminhcity",
      TRUE ~ old_province_key
    )
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    province_lookup_old %>% select(province_code, old_province_name, old_province_key),
    by = "old_province_key"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    province_34_mapping %>% select(province_code, new_province_name, new_province_key),
    by = "province_code"
  )

vn_map_34 <- vn_map_old %>%
  filter(!is.na(new_province_name)) %>%
  group_by(new_province_name, new_province_key) %>%
  summarise(
    old_provinces = paste(sort(unique(old_province_name)), collapse = " + "),
    geometry      = st_union(geometry),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  st_make_valid()

vn_map_34_expanded <- tidyr::crossing(survey_year = target_years, vn_map_34 %>% st_drop_geometry()) %>%
  left_join(vn_map_34 %>% select(new_province_key, geometry), by = "new_province_key") %>%
  st_as_sf()

vn_adoption_34_map <- vn_map_34_expanded %>%
  left_join(province_adoption_34, by = c("new_province_key", "new_province_name", "survey_year"))

make_island_polygon <- function(lon, lat, radius = 0.06, n = 30) {
  angle  <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n)
  coords <- cbind(lon + radius * cos(angle), lat + radius * sin(angle))
  st_polygon(list(coords))
}

island_polygons_base <- bind_rows(
  tibble(
    island_group = "Hoang Sa",
    lon    = c(111.2,111.5,111.8,112.1,112.4,112.7,111.7,112.0,112.3,112.9),
    lat    = c(16.1,16.4,16.2,16.5,16.7,16.3,16.8,15.9,16.1,16.6),
    radius = c(0.065,0.055,0.060,0.050,0.055,0.050,0.045,0.045,0.040,0.040)
  ),
  tibble(
    island_group = "Truong Sa",
    lon    = c(110.7,113.1,113.5,113.9,114.3,114.7,115.1,115.5,115.9,116.2,112.9,113.4,113.8,114.4,115.0,115.7),
    lat    = c(10.4,10.8,10.2,10.6,10.0,9.6,10.4,9.8,9.2,9.6,9.3,8.9,8.5,8.8,8.4,8.7),
    radius = c(0.060,0.045,0.060,0.050,0.055,0.050,0.045,0.045,0.040,0.040,0.045,0.040,0.040,0.040,0.035,0.035)
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    island_id = row_number(),
    geometry  = pmap(list(lon, lat, radius), ~ make_island_polygon(..1, ..2, ..3))
  ) %>%
  st_as_sf(crs = 4326)

island_polygons <- island_polygons_base %>%
  tidyr::crossing(survey_year = target_years) %>%
  st_as_sf()

island_labels <- tibble(
  island_group = c("Hoang Sa", "Truong Sa"),
  lon = c(113.5, 115.0),
  lat = c(16.5, 9.0)
) %>%
  tidyr::crossing(survey_year = target_years) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)
```

```{r page2-visual5, fig.width=12, fig.height=7, fig.cap=paste0('Figure 2.6: Equipment and innovation investment adoption rates across 34 consolidated provinces, ', base_year, ' and ', snapshot_year, '.')}
p_equipment_34 <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = vn_adoption_34_map, aes(fill = equipment_adoption_rate),
          colour = "#6B7280", linewidth = 0.02) +
  geom_sf(data = island_polygons, inherit.aes = FALSE,
          fill = "#12355B", colour = "white", linewidth = 0.12) +
  geom_sf_text(data = island_labels, aes(label = island_group), inherit.aes = FALSE,
               size = 3, colour = "#12355B", fontface = "bold") +
  facet_wrap(~ survey_year, ncol = 2, drop = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#E6F5F2", high = "#006D77",
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1), na.value = "#F1F5F9",
    name = "Equipment\nadoption rate"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = glue("{target_sector}: Equipment Investment Adoption By Province"),
    subtitle = "34-province map; share of firms with machinery and equipment investment, 2019 and 2023",
    caption  = "Adoption rate = firms with equipment investment / total firms in province-year."
  ) +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(102, 117), ylim = c(7, 24), expand = FALSE, datum = NA) +
  theme_void(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, colour = "#12355B"),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 10.5, colour = "#5B6F8A"),
    plot.caption    = element_text(size = 9, colour = "#6B7280", hjust = 0),
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12, colour = "#12355B"),
    panel.spacing   = unit(0.8, "lines"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.text     = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin     = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

p_equipment_34

p_innovation_34 <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = vn_adoption_34_map, aes(fill = innovation_adoption_rate),
          colour = "#6B7280", linewidth = 0.03) +
  geom_sf(data = island_polygons, inherit.aes = FALSE,
          fill = "#12355B", colour = "white", linewidth = 0.15) +
  geom_sf_text(data = island_labels, aes(label = island_group), inherit.aes = FALSE,
               size = 3, colour = "#12355B", fontface = "bold") +
  facet_wrap(~ survey_year, ncol = 2, drop = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#FFFBF0", high = "#E76F51",
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1), na.value = "#F1F5F9",
    name = "Innovation\nadoption rate"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = glue("{target_sector}: Innovation Investment Adoption By Province"),
    subtitle = "34-province map; share of firms with innovation investment, 2019 and 2023",
    caption  = "Adoption rate = firms with innovation investment / total firms in province-year."
  ) +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(102, 117), ylim = c(7, 24), expand = FALSE, datum = NA) +
  theme_void(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, colour = "#12355B"),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 10.5, colour = "#5B6F8A"),
    plot.caption    = element_text(size = 9, colour = "#6B7280", hjust = 0),
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12, colour = "#12355B"),
    panel.spacing   = unit(0.8, "lines"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.text     = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin     = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

p_innovation_34
```

```{r page2-visual5-interpretation, results='asis'}
top_equip_2023 <- province_adoption_34 %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
  arrange(desc(equipment_adoption_rate)) %>%
  slice(1)
top_innov_2023 <- province_adoption_34 %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
  arrange(desc(innovation_adoption_rate)) %>%
  slice(1)

equip_2019_row  <- province_adoption_34 %>%
  filter(survey_year == base_year, new_province_name == top_equip_2023$new_province_name)
equip_2019_rate <- if (nrow(equip_2019_row) > 0) equip_2019_row$equipment_adoption_rate[1] else NA_real_
equip_change_pp <- if (!is.na(equip_2019_rate)) {
  round((top_equip_2023$equipment_adoption_rate - equip_2019_rate) * 100, 1)
} else { NA_real_ }

equip_change_msg <- if_else(
  !is.na(equip_change_pp),
  glue(", {if_else(equip_change_pp >= 0, '+', '')}**{equip_change_pp} percentage points** change from {base_year}"),
  ""
)

# Innovation province change pp
innov_2019_row_map  <- province_adoption_34 %>%
  filter(survey_year == base_year, new_province_name == top_innov_2023$new_province_name)
innov_2019_rate_map <- if (nrow(innov_2019_row_map) > 0) innov_2019_row_map$innovation_adoption_rate[1] else NA_real_
innov_change_pp_map <- if (!is.na(innov_2019_rate_map)) {
  round((top_innov_2023$innovation_adoption_rate - innov_2019_rate_map) * 100, 1)
} else { NA_real_ }

innov_change_msg <- if_else(
  !is.na(innov_change_pp_map),
  glue(", {if_else(innov_change_pp_map >= 0, '+', '')}**{innov_change_pp_map} percentage points** change from {base_year}"),
  ""
)

# Concentration ratio: top province vs sector provincial average
national_equip_avg <- province_adoption_34 %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
  summarise(v = mean(equipment_adoption_rate, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% pull(v)

concentration_ratio <- safe_divide(top_equip_2023$equipment_adoption_rate, national_equip_avg)

concentration_msg <- case_when(
  is.na(concentration_ratio) ~ "",
  concentration_ratio >= 2.0 ~
    glue("The leading province's equipment adoption rate is more than double the provincial average ({percent(national_equip_avg, accuracy = 0.1)}), indicating high geographic concentration in technology adoption."),
  concentration_ratio >= 1.3 ~
    glue("The leading province's rate is {round(concentration_ratio, 1)}x the provincial average ({percent(national_equip_avg, accuracy = 0.1)}), suggesting moderate concentration with meaningful room for regional diffusion."),
  TRUE ~
    glue("The leading province's rate is close to the provincial average ({percent(national_equip_avg, accuracy = 0.1)}), indicating relatively uniform adoption across provinces.")
)

# Same or different leading province for equipment vs innovation
same_province_msg <- if_else(
  !is.na(top_equip_2023$new_province_name) && !is.na(top_innov_2023$new_province_name) &&
    top_equip_2023$new_province_name == top_innov_2023$new_province_name,
  glue("Both investment types are led by the same province ({top_equip_2023$new_province_name}), suggesting a single technology hub dominates the sector's geography."),
  "The leading province for equipment adoption differs from that for innovation adoption, suggesting distinct regional ecosystems for hardware and capability investment."
)

interp(glue(
  "In {snapshot_year}, the highest equipment adoption rate was in **{top_equip_2023$new_province_name}** ",
  "at **{percent(top_equip_2023$equipment_adoption_rate, accuracy = 0.1)}**{equip_change_msg}. ",
  "The highest innovation adoption rate was in **{top_innov_2023$new_province_name}** ",
  "at **{percent(top_innov_2023$innovation_adoption_rate, accuracy = 0.1)}**{innov_change_msg}. ",
  "{concentration_msg} ",
  "{same_province_msg}"
))
```

::: part-divider
<span>Part III: Productivity Dynamics</span>
:::

# 3. Productivity Dynamics & Five-Driver Decomposition

```{r s3-prep, include=FALSE}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 3: DECOMPOSITION DATA PREP
# Robust version for LP_decomposition_CGEsector.xlsx
# Handles: SectorName / CGE_sectorname, numeric or character Year,
# trailing spaces, multiple sheets, and missing AVG rows.
# ============================================================

have_decomp_file <- !is.null(params$decomp_path) &&
  params$decomp_path != "" &&
  file.exists(params$decomp_path)

decomp_raw <- if (have_decomp_file) {
  readxl::excel_sheets(params$decomp_path) %>%
    set_names() %>%
    purrr::map_dfr(~ readxl::read_excel(params$decomp_path, sheet = .x), .id = "sheet_name")
} else {
  tibble()
}

clean_txt <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    as.character() %>%
    stringr::str_squish() %>%
    stringr::str_to_lower()
}

find_first_col <- function(data, candidates) {
  hit <- intersect(candidates, names(data))
  if (length(hit) == 0) NA_character_ else hit[1]
}

sector_col <- find_first_col(
  decomp_raw,
  c("CGE_sectorname", "CGE_sector", "SectorName", "sector", "Sector", "sector_name", "CGE sector name")
)

year_col <- find_first_col(
  decomp_raw,
  c("Year", "year", "survey_year", "Survey year")
)

required_decomp_cols <- c(
  "gKD", "g_tech_change", "g_effi_change",
  "g_equip_change", "g_inno_change",
  "gTFP", "gLP_macro", "gLP_check"
)

decomp_sector <- if (nrow(decomp_raw) > 0 && !is.na(sector_col) && !is.na(year_col)) {
  decomp_raw %>%
    mutate(
      sector_match_key = clean_txt(.data[[sector_col]]),
      target_match_key = clean_txt(target_sector)
    ) %>%
    filter(sector_match_key == target_match_key) %>%
    rename(Year = all_of(year_col)) %>%
    mutate(Year = suppressWarnings(as.integer(as.character(Year))))
} else {
  tibble()
}

if (nrow(decomp_sector) > 0) {
  for (cc in required_decomp_cols) {
    if (!cc %in% names(decomp_sector)) decomp_sector[[cc]] <- NA_real_
  }
  decomp_sector <- decomp_sector %>%
    mutate(across(all_of(required_decomp_cols), ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.x))))
}

decomp_years <- if (nrow(decomp_sector) > 0 && "Year" %in% names(decomp_sector)) {
  decomp_sector %>%
    filter(Year %in% 2020:2023) %>%
    select(Year, all_of(required_decomp_cols)) %>%
    arrange(Year)
} else {
  tibble(
    Year = integer(),
    gKD = numeric(),
    g_tech_change = numeric(),
    g_effi_change = numeric(),
    g_equip_change = numeric(),
    g_inno_change = numeric(),
    gTFP = numeric(),
    gLP_macro = numeric(),
    gLP_check = numeric()
  )
}

decomp_avg <- if (nrow(decomp_years) > 0) {
  decomp_years %>%
    summarise(
      across(all_of(required_decomp_cols), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(all_of(required_decomp_cols), ~ ifelse(is.nan(.x), NA_real_, .x)))
} else {
  tibble(
    gKD = NA_real_,
    g_tech_change = NA_real_,
    g_effi_change = NA_real_,
    g_equip_change = NA_real_,
    g_inno_change = NA_real_,
    gTFP = NA_real_,
    gLP_macro = NA_real_,
    gLP_check = NA_real_
  )
}

have_decomp <- nrow(decomp_avg) == 1 &&
  is.finite(decomp_avg$gLP_macro) &&
  !is.na(decomp_avg$gLP_macro)

# Diagnostic objects for quick checking in console if needed:
# decomp_sector_match_n should be 5 rows for one sector-year panel, usually 2019–2023.
decomp_sector_match_n <- nrow(decomp_sector)
decomp_years_match_n <- nrow(decomp_years)
decomp_available_sectors <- if (!is.na(sector_col) && nrow(decomp_raw) > 0) {
  decomp_raw %>% distinct(.data[[sector_col]]) %>% arrange(.data[[sector_col]])
} else tibble()

if (have_decomp) {
  drv_named <- tibble(
    key = c("KD", "TC", "CE", "ZE1", "ZE2"),
    name = c("Capital Deepening", "Technology Frontier Change", "Efficiency Change", "Hard-Tech Diffusion", "Soft-Tech Diffusion"),
    label = c("Capital
Deepening", "Technology
Frontier Change", "Efficiency
Change", "Hard-Tech
Diffusion", "Soft-Tech
Diffusion"),
    value = c(decomp_avg$gKD, decomp_avg$g_tech_change, decomp_avg$g_effi_change, decomp_avg$g_equip_change, decomp_avg$g_inno_change)
  ) %>%
    mutate(
      share     = value / decomp_avg$gLP_check,
      share_pct = share * 100
    )

  dominant_driver <- drv_named %>% arrange(desc(abs(value))) %>% slice(1)
  diffusion_total <- decomp_avg$g_equip_change + decomp_avg$g_inno_change
  diffusion_share  <- safe_divide(diffusion_total, decomp_avg$gLP_check)
  frontier_share   <- safe_divide(decomp_avg$g_tech_change, decomp_avg$gLP_check)
  capital_share    <- safe_divide(decomp_avg$gKD, decomp_avg$gLP_check)
  efficiency_share <- safe_divide(decomp_avg$g_effi_change, decomp_avg$gLP_check)

  regime_decomp <- if (is.na(decomp_avg$gLP_check) || decomp_avg$gLP_check <= 0) {
    "contraction"
  } else {
    case_when(
      capital_share > 0.50 ~ "capital-led",
      frontier_share > 0.40 ~ "frontier-led",
      diffusion_share > 0.40 ~ "diffusion-led",
      efficiency_share > 0.40 ~ "efficiency-led",
      TRUE ~ "mixed"
    )
  }
} else {
  drv_named <- tibble()
  dominant_driver <- tibble(name = "not available", value = NA_real_, share = NA_real_)
  diffusion_total <- diffusion_share <- frontier_share <- capital_share <- efficiency_share <- NA_real_
  regime_decomp <- "unavailable"
}
```


## 3.1 Average Driver Contributions

<p class="section-desc">
Waterfall decomposition of average annual labour productivity growth into five drivers. The chart identifies the dominant growth engine and the balance between frontier expansion and technology diffusion.
</p>

```{r s3-waterfall-data, include=FALSE}
if (have_decomp) {
  wf <- drv_named %>%
    mutate(
      idx = row_number(),
      x_start = idx - 0.4,
      x_end = idx + 0.4,
      cum_before = cumsum(lag(value, default = 0)),
      cum_after = cumsum(value),
      y_bottom = pmin(cum_before, cum_after),
      y_top = pmax(cum_before, cum_after),
      label_y = pmax(cum_before, cum_after) + 0.15,
      bar_label = paste0(ifelse(value >= 0, "+", ""), round(value, 1),
                         "\n(", round(share_pct, 0), "%)")
    )

  total_idx <- nrow(wf) + 1

  total_bar <- tibble(
    key = "TOTAL", name = "Total Labour Productivity Growth", label = "Total Labour\nProductivity Growth",
    value = decomp_avg$gLP_check, share_pct = 100, idx = total_idx,
    x_start = total_idx - 0.4, x_end = total_idx + 0.4,
    cum_before = 0, cum_after = decomp_avg$gLP_check,
    y_bottom = pmin(0, decomp_avg$gLP_check), y_top = pmax(0, decomp_avg$gLP_check),
    label_y = pmax(0, decomp_avg$gLP_check) + 0.15,
    bar_label = paste0(ifelse(decomp_avg$gLP_check >= 0, "+", ""),
                       round(decomp_avg$gLP_check, 1), "\n(100%)")
  )

  wf_all <- bind_rows(wf, total_bar)

  connectors <- wf %>%
    mutate(x = x_end, xend = lead(x_start, default = total_idx - 0.4), y = cum_after, yend = cum_after) %>%
    select(x, xend, y, yend)

  wf_axis_top <- max(c(wf_all$y_top, 0), na.rm = TRUE) * 1.25 + 0.5
  wf_axis_bot <- min(c(wf_all$y_bottom, 0), na.rm = TRUE) - 0.5
}
```

```{r s3-waterfall-plot, results='asis', fig.width=12, fig.height=6.5, fig.cap='Figure 3.1. Average driver contributions to annual labour productivity growth, 2020 to 2023. Each bar shows a driver contribution in percentage points; the right-hand bar is the total.'}
if (have_decomp) {
  driver_cols <- c("KD" = "#2A9D8F", "TC" = "#12355B", "CE" = "#4CAF50", "ZE1" = "#4A6FA5", "ZE2" = "#F4A261", "TOTAL" = "#12355B")

  ggplot() +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "#6B7280", linewidth = 0.6) +
    geom_segment(data = connectors, aes(x = x, xend = xend, y = y, yend = yend),
                 linetype = "dashed", colour = "#8898A7", linewidth = 0.55) +
    geom_rect(data = wf_all, aes(xmin = x_start, xmax = x_end, ymin = y_bottom, ymax = y_top, fill = key),
              colour = "white", linewidth = 0.6) +
    geom_text(data = wf_all,
              aes(x = idx, y = label_y,
                  label = bar_label,
                  colour = key),
              fontface = "bold", size = 4.2, lineheight = 0.92, vjust = 0) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = driver_cols) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = driver_cols) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(wf$idx, total_idx), labels = c(wf$label, "Total Labour\nProductivity Growth"),
                       expand = expansion(add = c(0.3, 0.3))) +
    scale_y_continuous(name = "Contribution to annual labour productivity growth (percentage points)",
                       limits = c(min(0, wf_axis_bot), wf_axis_top),
                       breaks = pretty(c(wf_axis_bot, wf_axis_top), n = 8),
                       expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))) +
    labs(title = glue("{target_sector}: Drivers of Annual Labour Productivity Growth ({base_year} to {snapshot_year})"),
         subtitle = "Average annual contribution to labour productivity growth (percentage points)",
         x = NULL) +
    theme_sector() +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "#12355B", size = 11, lineheight = 0.95),
          panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())
} else {
  cat("<div class='data-note'>Decomposition data for this sector is unavailable; the waterfall chart has been skipped.</div>")
}
```

```{r s3-waterfall-interpretation, results='asis'}
if (have_decomp) {
  diff_frontier_msg <- case_when(
    !is.na(diffusion_share) && !is.na(frontier_share) && diffusion_share > frontier_share + 0.10 ~
      glue("Diffusion-related drivers contributed **{percent(diffusion_share, accuracy = 1)}** of growth vs **{percent(frontier_share, accuracy = 1)}** from frontier change, meaning capability is spreading across the firm distribution faster than leading firms are advancing the boundary."),
    !is.na(diffusion_share) && !is.na(frontier_share) && frontier_share > diffusion_share + 0.10 ~
      glue("Technology Frontier Change contributed **{percent(frontier_share, accuracy = 1)}** of growth vs **{percent(diffusion_share, accuracy = 1)}** from diffusion, meaning leading firms are advancing faster than the broader sector can absorb , a pattern that tends to widen the frontier-laggard gap over time."),
    TRUE ~
      glue("Frontier change (**{percent(frontier_share, accuracy = 1)}**) and diffusion (**{percent(diffusion_share, accuracy = 1)}**) contributed in broadly similar magnitudes, indicating a relatively balanced productivity structure with neither channel dominating.")
  )

  regime_msg <- case_when(
    regime_decomp == "capital-led" ~
      "The sector is best characterised as **capital-led**: growth relies heavily on rising capital per worker and faces diminishing returns unless complemented by capability upgrading.",
    regime_decomp == "frontier-led" ~
      "The sector is best characterised as **frontier-led**: leading firms are carrying a large share of productivity improvement, creating risk of widening within-sector gaps if diffusion mechanisms do not keep pace.",
    regime_decomp == "diffusion-led" ~
      "The sector is best characterised as **diffusion-led**: adoption and capability spread are central to productivity growth, meaning gains are reaching a wider set of firms rather than concentrating at the frontier.",
    regime_decomp == "efficiency-led" ~
      "The sector is best characterised as **efficiency-led**: resource allocation and operational performance are the main growth channels, which are real but bounded ; once easy efficiency gains are captured, further growth requires a different engine.",
    regime_decomp == "mixed" ~
      "The sector has a **mixed** growth structure, with no single driver fully dominating, meaning no single policy lever in isolation is likely to materially shift the productivity trajectory.",
    TRUE ~ "The sector's growth regime could not be classified reliably from the available decomposition data."
  )

  interp(glue(
    "{target_sector} recorded **{round(decomp_avg$gLP_check, 2)} percentage points** of average annual labour productivity growth between **2020 and 2023**. ",
    "The largest contributor was **{dominant_driver$name}**, adding **{round(dominant_driver$value, 2)} percentage points** (**{percent(dominant_driver$share, accuracy = 1)}** of total growth). ",
    "Capital Deepening contributed **{round(decomp_avg$gKD, 2)} percentage points** and Total Factor Productivity contributed **{round(decomp_avg$gTFP, 2)} percentage points**.",
    "\n\n",
    "{diff_frontier_msg} {regime_msg} ",
    "This diagnosis matters for policy because the dominant driver identifies whether future productivity gains are more likely to come from capital accumulation, frontier innovation, diffusion mechanisms or efficiency upgrading."
  ))
}
```

## 3.2 Year-by-Year Driver Dynamics

<p class="section-desc">
Stacked decomposition of annual labour productivity growth. This visual shows whether the sector's growth structure remained stable or shifted over time.
</p>

```{r s3-stacked-plot, results='asis', fig.width=11, fig.height=6, fig.cap='Figure 3.2. Year-by-year decomposition of labour productivity growth, 2020 to 2023. Bars stack the five drivers; the red line tracks total labour productivity growth.'}
if (nrow(decomp_years) >= 1) {
  yearly <- decomp_years %>%
    filter(Year %in% 2020:2023) %>%
    select(Year, `Capital Deepening` = gKD, `Technology Frontier Change` = g_tech_change,
           `Efficiency Change` = g_effi_change, `Hard-Tech Diffusion` = g_equip_change,
           `Soft-Tech Diffusion` = g_inno_change, total = gLP_check) %>%
    pivot_longer(-c(Year, total), names_to = "Driver", values_to = "pp") %>%
    mutate(Driver = factor(Driver, levels = c("Capital Deepening", "Technology Frontier Change",
                                              "Efficiency Change", "Hard-Tech Diffusion", "Soft-Tech Diffusion")))

  driver_pal <- c("Capital Deepening" = "#2A9D8F", "Technology Frontier Change" = "#12355B",
                  "Efficiency Change" = "#4CAF50", "Hard-Tech Diffusion" = "#4A6FA5",
                  "Soft-Tech Diffusion" = "#F4A261")

  totals_df <- yearly %>% distinct(Year, total)

  ggplot(yearly, aes(x = factor(Year), y = pp, fill = Driver)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.7, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "#6B7280", linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_line(data = totals_df, aes(x = factor(Year), y = total, group = 1),
              inherit.aes = FALSE, colour = "#E63946", linewidth = 1.3) +
    geom_point(data = totals_df, aes(x = factor(Year), y = total),
               inherit.aes = FALSE, colour = "#E63946", size = 3.5) +
    geom_text(data = totals_df, aes(x = factor(Year), y = total,
                                    label = paste0(ifelse(total >= 0, "+", ""), round(total, 1), " pp")),
              inherit.aes = FALSE, vjust = -0.9, fontface = "bold", colour = "#E63946", size = 3.6) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = driver_pal) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = number_format(accuracy = 0.1), expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.2))) +
    labs(title = glue("{target_sector}: Year-by-Year Driver Decomposition"),
         subtitle = "Stacked bars show each driver's contribution (percentage points). Red line: total labour productivity growth.",
         x = NULL, y = "Contribution (percentage points)", fill = "Driver") +
    theme_sector() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
} else {
  cat("<div class='data-note'>Year-by-year decomposition data unavailable for this sector.</div>")
}
```

```{r s3-stacked-interpretation, results='asis'}
if (nrow(decomp_years) >= 1) {
  LP_best_year <- decomp_years$Year[which.max(decomp_years$gLP_check)]
  LP_worst_year <- decomp_years$Year[which.min(decomp_years$gLP_check)]
  LP_best_val <- max(decomp_years$gLP_check, na.rm = TRUE)
  LP_worst_val <- min(decomp_years$gLP_check, na.rm = TRUE)

  yr_largest <- function(col) {
    vals <- decomp_years[[col]]
    if (all(is.na(vals))) return("not available")
    decomp_years$Year[which.max(abs(vals))]
  }

  yr_KD <- yr_largest("gKD")
  yr_TC <- yr_largest("g_tech_change")
  yr_CE <- yr_largest("g_effi_change")
  yr_Z1 <- yr_largest("g_equip_change")
  yr_Z2 <- yr_largest("g_inno_change")

  diffusion_2020 <- decomp_years %>% filter(Year == 2020) %>%
    summarise(v = sum(g_equip_change, g_inno_change, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% pull(v)
  diffusion_2023 <- decomp_years %>% filter(Year == 2023) %>%
    summarise(v = sum(g_equip_change, g_inno_change, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% pull(v)

  diffusion_trend_msg <- case_when(
    is.na(diffusion_2020) | is.na(diffusion_2023) ~
      "The diffusion trend across the period could not be measured.",
    diffusion_2023 > diffusion_2020 + 0.5 ~
      glue("Combined diffusion contributions strengthened from **{round(diffusion_2020, 1)} percentage points** in 2020 to **{round(diffusion_2023, 1)} percentage points** in 2023, suggesting broader technology absorption as the period progressed."),
    diffusion_2023 < diffusion_2020 - 0.5 ~
      glue("Combined diffusion contributions declined from **{round(diffusion_2020, 1)} percentage points** in 2020 to **{round(diffusion_2023, 1)} percentage points** in 2023, indicating that adoption gains were concentrated in the early years."),
    TRUE ~
      glue("Diffusion contributions remained broadly stable across the period (from **{round(diffusion_2020, 1)} percentage points** in 2020 to **{round(diffusion_2023, 1)} percentage points** in 2023), indicating a consistent but not accelerating absorption capacity.")
  )

  lp_range <- LP_best_val - LP_worst_val

  volatility_msg <- case_when(
    lp_range > 5 ~
      "The range between the best and worst year exceeds 5 percentage points, indicating high year-to-year volatility in the sector's productivity trajectory.",
    lp_range > 2 ~
      "Moderate year-to-year variation in labour productivity growth suggests the sector's performance is sensitive to annual shocks rather than following a smooth structural trend.",
    TRUE ~
      "Relatively stable year-to-year variation indicates that the sector's growth structure is consistent rather than driven by year-specific shocks."
  )

  interp(glue(
    "Labour productivity growth was highest in **{LP_best_year}** at **{round(LP_best_val, 2)} percentage points** and lowest in **{LP_worst_year}** at **{round(LP_worst_val, 2)} percentage points**. ",
    "{volatility_msg} ",
    "Capital Deepening made its largest annual contribution in **{yr_KD}**, Technology Frontier Change in **{yr_TC}**, Efficiency Change in **{yr_CE}**, Hard-Tech Diffusion in **{yr_Z1}**, and Soft-Tech Diffusion in **{yr_Z2}**.",
    "\n\n",
    "{diffusion_trend_msg} ",
    "The year-by-year pattern matters because a stable and broad-based contribution from diffusion is usually more sustainable than growth concentrated in a single year or a single channel."
  ))
}
```

## 3.3 Conditional Productivity Frontier

<p class="section-desc">
Quantile-regression frontier comparing 2019 and 2023. The frontier shows the productivity level reached by top-performing firms at comparable capital intensity.
</p>

```{r s3-frontier-prep, include=FALSE}
TARGET_SECTOR <- target_sector
YEAR_EARLY <- 2019
YEAR_LATE <- 2023
TAU <- 0.90
TAU_LO <- 0.80
TAU_HI <- 0.99
DF_SPLINE <- 3
UNIT_LABEL <- "mn VND"
UNIT_DIV <- 1
OUTLIER_CAP <- 0.97

frontier_sector_df <- sector_df %>%
  filter(survey_year %in% c(YEAR_EARLY, YEAR_LATE),
         !is.na(revenue_per_worker), !is.na(capital_intensity),
         revenue_per_worker > 0, capital_intensity > 0) %>%
  mutate(y_pw = revenue_per_worker / UNIT_DIV,
         k_pw = capital_intensity / UNIT_DIV) %>%
  group_by(survey_year) %>%
  filter(y_pw <= quantile(y_pw, OUTLIER_CAP, na.rm = TRUE),
         k_pw <= quantile(k_pw, OUTLIER_CAP, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

n_early <- sum(frontier_sector_df$survey_year == YEAR_EARLY)
n_late <- sum(frontier_sector_df$survey_year == YEAR_LATE)

fit_one_quantile <- function(yr_data, tau, df_spline, n_grid) {
  fit <- quantreg::rq(y_pw ~ splines::ns(k_pw, df = df_spline), tau = tau, data = yr_data)
  k_grid <- seq(min(yr_data$k_pw, na.rm = TRUE), max(yr_data$k_pw, na.rm = TRUE), length.out = n_grid)
  tibble(k_pw = k_grid, y = as.numeric(predict(fit, newdata = data.frame(k_pw = k_grid))))
}

fit_frontier <- function(yr_data, tau, df_spline = 5, n_grid = 200) {
  main <- fit_one_quantile(yr_data, tau, df_spline, n_grid)
  lo <- fit_one_quantile(yr_data, TAU_LO, df_spline, n_grid)
  hi <- fit_one_quantile(yr_data, TAU_HI, df_spline, n_grid)
  tibble(k_pw = main$k_pw, fit = main$y, lower = lo$y, upper = hi$y)
}

front_early <- frontier_sector_df %>% filter(survey_year == YEAR_EARLY) %>%
  fit_frontier(TAU, DF_SPLINE) %>% mutate(year = YEAR_EARLY)
front_late <- frontier_sector_df %>% filter(survey_year == YEAR_LATE) %>%
  fit_frontier(TAU, DF_SPLINE) %>% mutate(year = YEAR_LATE)

avg_pts <- frontier_sector_df %>%
  group_by(survey_year) %>%
  summarise(
    k_avg = if ("labour_headcount_end" %in% names(frontier_sector_df)) weighted.mean(k_pw, w = labour_headcount_end, na.rm = TRUE) else mean(k_pw, na.rm = TRUE),
    y_avg = if ("labour_headcount_end" %in% names(frontier_sector_df)) weighted.mean(y_pw, w = labour_headcount_end, na.rm = TRUE) else mean(y_pw, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

COL_EARLY <- "#12355B"
COL_LATE <- "#2A9D8F"
label_early_f <- glue("Frontier {YEAR_EARLY}")
label_late_f <- glue("Frontier {YEAR_LATE}")
label_early_av <- glue("Industry average {YEAR_EARLY}")
label_late_av <- glue("Industry average {YEAR_LATE}")

colour_map <- c(setNames(COL_EARLY, label_early_f), setNames(COL_LATE, label_late_f),
                setNames(COL_EARLY, label_early_av), setNames(COL_LATE, label_late_av))
linetype_map <- c(setNames("solid", label_early_f), setNames("solid", label_late_f),
                  setNames("blank", label_early_av), setNames("blank", label_late_av))
shape_map <- c(setNames(NA, label_early_f), setNames(NA, label_late_f),
               setNames(16L, label_early_av), setNames(16L, label_late_av))
```

```{r s3-frontier-plot, fig.width=11, fig.height=6.5, fig.cap='Figure 3.3. Conditional productivity frontier, 2019 and 2023. The frontier line is the 90th-percentile quantile regression with a natural spline; the shaded band spans the 80th to 99th percentile frontier zone.'}
if (n_early >= 30 && n_late >= 30) {
  ggplot() +
    geom_ribbon(data = front_late, aes(x = k_pw, ymin = lower, ymax = upper), fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.55) +
    geom_ribbon(data = front_early, aes(x = k_pw, ymin = lower, ymax = upper), fill = "grey60", alpha = 0.45) +
    geom_line(data = front_early, aes(x = k_pw, y = fit, colour = label_early_f, linetype = label_early_f), linewidth = 1.0) +
    geom_line(data = front_late, aes(x = k_pw, y = fit, colour = label_late_f, linetype = label_late_f), linewidth = 1.0) +
    geom_point(data = avg_pts %>% filter(survey_year == YEAR_EARLY),
               aes(x = k_avg, y = y_avg, colour = label_early_av, shape = label_early_av), size = 3.5) +
    geom_point(data = avg_pts %>% filter(survey_year == YEAR_LATE),
               aes(x = k_avg, y = y_avg, colour = label_late_av, shape = label_late_av), size = 3.5) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = colour_map, breaks = c(label_late_f, label_early_f, label_late_av, label_early_av)) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = linetype_map, breaks = c(label_late_f, label_early_f, label_late_av, label_early_av)) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = shape_map, breaks = c(label_late_f, label_early_f, label_late_av, label_early_av)) +
    guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linetype = c("solid", "solid", "blank", "blank"),
                                                      shape = c(NA, NA, 16, 16), size = c(1, 1, 3, 3))),
           linetype = "none", shape = "none") +
    labs(
      title = glue("Conditional frontier for {TARGET_SECTOR} {YEAR_EARLY}–{YEAR_LATE}"),
      x = glue("Real capital per worker ({UNIT_LABEL}, 2019 prices)"),
      y = glue("Real output per worker ({UNIT_LABEL}, 2019 prices)"),
      colour = NULL,
      caption = glue("Note: Frontier line is the {round(TAU * 100)}th-percentile quantile regression with natural spline ",
                     "(df = {DF_SPLINE}). Shaded band spans the {round(TAU_LO * 100)}th–{round(TAU_HI * 100)}th percentile ",
                     "frontier zone. Industry average is labour-weighted mean. Source: GSO Enterprise Survey, 2019–2023.")
    ) +
    theme_sector() +
    theme(plot.caption    = element_text(size = 9, colour = "#6B7280", hjust = 0, margin = margin(t = 10)),
          legend.position  = "bottom",
          legend.text      = element_text(size = 10),
          legend.key.width = unit(1.8, "cm"))
} else {
  cat("<div class='data-note'>Conditional frontier data unavailable or insufficient for this sector (fewer than 30 firms in one or both years).</div>")
}
```

```{r s3-frontier-interpretation, results='asis'}
if (n_early >= 30 && n_late >= 30) {
  avg_early <- avg_pts %>% filter(survey_year == YEAR_EARLY)
  avg_late <- avg_pts %>% filter(survey_year == YEAR_LATE)

  avg_lp_change <- safe_divide(avg_late$y_avg[1], avg_early$y_avg[1]) - 1
  avg_cap_change <- safe_divide(avg_late$k_avg[1], avg_early$k_avg[1]) - 1

  frontier_mid_early <- front_early %>%
    filter(k_pw >= quantile(k_pw, 0.45, na.rm = TRUE), k_pw <= quantile(k_pw, 0.55, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    summarise(v = mean(fit, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% pull(v)

  frontier_mid_late <- front_late %>%
    filter(k_pw >= quantile(k_pw, 0.45, na.rm = TRUE), k_pw <= quantile(k_pw, 0.55, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    summarise(v = mean(fit, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% pull(v)

  frontier_mid_change <- safe_divide(frontier_mid_late, frontier_mid_early) - 1

  frontier_msg <- if_else(!is.na(frontier_mid_change) && frontier_mid_change > 0,
                          glue("The conditional frontier shifted upward by approximately **{percent(frontier_mid_change, accuracy = 0.1)}** around the mid-range of capital intensity."),
                          "The conditional frontier did not clearly shift upward around the mid-range of capital intensity.")

  avg_msg <- if_else(!is.na(avg_lp_change) && avg_lp_change > 0,
                     glue("The labour-weighted industry average output per worker also increased by **{percent(avg_lp_change, accuracy = 0.1)}**, while average capital per worker changed by **{percent(avg_cap_change, accuracy = 0.1)}**."),
                     glue("The labour-weighted industry average output per worker did not materially improve, despite a **{percent(avg_cap_change, accuracy = 0.1)}** change in average capital per worker."))

  structural_interp_msg <- case_when(
    is.na(frontier_mid_change) ~
      "The frontier shift cannot be assessed from available data.",
    frontier_mid_change > 0.10 & !is.na(avg_lp_change) & avg_lp_change > 0 ~
      glue("Both the frontier and the industry average improved. If the frontier outpaced the average (which is consistent with a wide and stable leader-laggard gap) it signals that diffusion is not fully keeping pace with frontier advancement."),
    frontier_mid_change > 0.10 & (is.na(avg_lp_change) | avg_lp_change <= 0) ~
      "The frontier shifted upward but the industry average did not improve materially : the clearest signal of a diffusion constraint: leading firms are advancing while most firms are not.",
    frontier_mid_change <= 0 & !is.na(avg_lp_change) & avg_lp_change > 0 ~
      "The industry average improved despite a flat or declining frontier, consistent with catch-up by laggard firms rather than frontier-driven growth.",
    TRUE ~
      "Neither the frontier nor the industry average showed clear improvement, suggesting the sector's productivity structure was broadly stagnant over the period."
  )

  interp(glue(
    "{frontier_msg} ",
    "{avg_msg} ",
    "{structural_interp_msg} ",
    "Structurally, the chart compares frontier movement with the average firm, showing whether productivity gains are concentrated at the top or absorbing more broadly. ",
    "A persistent distance between the frontier and the average is consistent with a capability diffusion gap rather than a simple lack of capital."
  ))
}
```

::: part-divider
<span>Part IV: Summary and Policy Insights</span>
:::

# 4. Summary Highlights & Policy Insights

## 4.1 Summary Highlights

<p class="section-desc">
Concise synthesis of the sector structure, technology adoption and productivity decomposition results. Each block reports a key numerical signal and its structural interpretation.
</p>

```{r s4-prep, include=FALSE}
economy_snapshot <- all_sector_year %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
  summarise(total_revenue = sum(revenue, na.rm = TRUE), total_labour = sum(labour, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

sector_snapshot <- sector_year %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
  summarise(revenue = sum(revenue, na.rm = TRUE), labour = sum(labour, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

rev_share_econ <- safe_divide(sector_snapshot$revenue, economy_snapshot$total_revenue)
lab_share_econ <- safe_divide(sector_snapshot$labour, economy_snapshot$total_labour)

sector_ranks_snapshot <- all_sector_year %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
  mutate(rank_revenue = min_rank(desc(revenue)), rank_labour = min_rank(desc(labour))) %>%
  filter(CGE_sectorname == target_sector)

ownership_summary_5yr <- sector_df %>%
  filter(survey_year %in% analysis_years, !is.na(firm_ownership_type_factor)) %>%
  group_by(firm_ownership_type_factor) %>%
  summarise(revenue = sum(total_net_revenue, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(revenue_share = safe_divide(revenue, sum(revenue, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  arrange(desc(revenue_share))

dominant_owner <- if (nrow(ownership_summary_5yr) > 0) as.character(ownership_summary_5yr$firm_ownership_type_factor[1]) else "not available"
dominant_owner_share <- if (nrow(ownership_summary_5yr) > 0) ownership_summary_5yr$revenue_share[1] else NA_real_

adoption_snapshot <- sector_year %>%
  filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
  summarise(equip_rate = mean(equipment_adoption_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
            innov_rate = mean(innovation_adoption_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
            total_investment = sum(total_investment, na.rm = TRUE),
            innovation_investment = sum(innovation_investment, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")

adoption_base <- sector_year %>%
  filter(survey_year == base_year) %>%
  summarise(equip_rate_base = mean(equipment_adoption_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
            innov_rate_base = mean(innovation_adoption_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")

equip_rate_change_pp <- (adoption_snapshot$equip_rate - adoption_base$equip_rate_base) * 100
innov_rate_change_pp <- (adoption_snapshot$innov_rate - adoption_base$innov_rate_base) * 100
innovation_investment_share <- safe_divide(adoption_snapshot$innovation_investment, adoption_snapshot$total_investment)

frontier_gap_latest <- if (exists("frontier_year_metrics")) {
  tmp <- frontier_year_metrics %>% filter(survey_year == snapshot_year, Metric == "Labour productivity")
  if (nrow(tmp) > 0) tmp$Ratio[1] else NA_real_
} else {
  sector_df %>%
    mutate(firm_productivity = safe_divide(total_net_revenue, labour_headcount_end)) %>%
    filter(survey_year == snapshot_year, labour_headcount_end > 0, firm_productivity > 0) %>%
    mutate(productivity_rank = percent_rank(firm_productivity)) %>%
    summarise(top = median(firm_productivity[productivity_rank >= 0.97], na.rm = TRUE),
              rest = median(firm_productivity[productivity_rank < 0.97], na.rm = TRUE),
              ratio = safe_divide(top, rest), .groups = "drop") %>%
    pull(ratio)
}

top_equip_province <- if (exists("province_adoption_34")) {
  province_adoption_34 %>% filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
    arrange(desc(equipment_adoption_rate)) %>% slice(1)
} else tibble(new_province_name = "not available", equipment_adoption_rate = NA_real_)

top_innov_province <- if (exists("province_adoption_34")) {
  province_adoption_34 %>% filter(survey_year == snapshot_year) %>%
    arrange(desc(innovation_adoption_rate)) %>% slice(1)
} else tibble(new_province_name = "not available", innovation_adoption_rate = NA_real_)

lp_growth_avg <- if (have_decomp) decomp_avg$gLP_check else NA_real_
dominant_name <- if (have_decomp) dominant_driver$name else "not available"
dominant_value <- if (have_decomp) dominant_driver$value else NA_real_
dominant_share <- if (have_decomp) dominant_driver$share else NA_real_

# ── C1: Frontier gap trend ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
frontier_gap_base <- tryCatch({
  sector_df %>%
    mutate(firm_productivity = safe_divide(total_net_revenue, labour_headcount_end)) %>%
    filter(survey_year == base_year, labour_headcount_end > 0, firm_productivity > 0) %>%
    mutate(productivity_rank = percent_rank(firm_productivity)) %>%
    summarise(top  = median(firm_productivity[productivity_rank >= 0.97], na.rm = TRUE),
              rest = median(firm_productivity[productivity_rank <  0.97], na.rm = TRUE),
              ratio = safe_divide(top, rest), .groups = "drop") %>%
    pull(ratio)
}, error = function(e) NA_real_)

frontier_trend_msg <- case_when(
  is.na(frontier_gap_base) | is.na(frontier_gap_latest) ~
    "not available across both years",
  frontier_gap_latest > frontier_gap_base * 1.15 ~
    glue("widened from **{round(frontier_gap_base, 1)}x** in {base_year} to **{round(frontier_gap_latest, 1)}x** in {snapshot_year} , indicating that diffusion from leading firms to the wider sector has not kept pace with frontier advancement"),
  frontier_gap_latest < frontier_gap_base * 0.85 ~
    glue("narrowed from **{round(frontier_gap_base, 1)}x** in {base_year} to **{round(frontier_gap_latest, 1)}x** in {snapshot_year} , indicating that laggard firms are catching up and diffusion mechanisms are working"),
  TRUE ~
    glue("remained broadly stable between **{round(frontier_gap_base, 1)}x** in {base_year} and **{round(frontier_gap_latest, 1)}x** in {snapshot_year} , suggesting the frontier-laggard structure is entrenched rather than converging")
)

# ── C2: HD / SD / efficiency split ────────────────────────────────────────────
hd_value   <- if (have_decomp) decomp_avg$g_equip_change else NA_real_
sd_value   <- if (have_decomp) decomp_avg$g_inno_change  else NA_real_
effi_value <- if (have_decomp) decomp_avg$g_effi_change  else NA_real_

diffusion_type_msg <- case_when(
  is.na(hd_value) | is.na(sd_value) ~ "",
  hd_value > sd_value + 0.5 ~
    "Within adoption, equipment investment (hard-tech diffusion) was the larger channel , pointing to capital-embodied upgrading that may not yet be matched by organisational and capability development.",
  sd_value > hd_value + 0.5 ~
    "Within adoption, innovation investment (soft-tech diffusion) was the larger channel , a relatively advanced pattern where capability and management practices are outpacing physical equipment as productivity drivers.",
  TRUE ~
    "Hard-tech (equipment) and soft-tech (innovation) diffusion contributed in roughly similar proportions, indicating a balanced adoption profile."
)

effi_sign_msg <- case_when(
  is.na(effi_value)  ~ "",
  effi_value < -0.5  ~
    glue("Efficiency change was negative (**{round(effi_value, 2)} percentage points**): laggard firms are falling further behind best-practice operations, acting as a drag on aggregate productivity that management and organisational improvement could address."),
  effi_value >  0.5  ~
    glue("Efficiency change was positive (**{round(effi_value, 2)} percentage points**): laggard firms are closing the gap to frontier operating standards, a healthy sign that soft capabilities are diffusing alongside investment."),
  TRUE               ~
    glue("Efficiency change was near zero (**{round(effi_value, 2)} percentage points**): laggard firm performance relative to best practice has not materially improved or deteriorated.")
)
```

```{r s4-highlights, results='asis'}
highlight_block <- function(title, body) {
  body <- gsub("\\*\\*(.*?)\\*\\*", "<strong>\\1</strong>", body, perl = TRUE)
  paste0('<div class="highlight-block"><span class="highlight-title">', title,
         '</span><div class="highlight-body">', body, '</div></div>')
}

# ── Safe display strings for highlight blocks (NA-proof) ─────────────────────
frontier_gap_highlight_str <- if (!is.na(frontier_gap_latest)) glue("{round(frontier_gap_latest, 1)}x") else "not available"
lp_growth_str       <- if (!is.na(lp_growth_avg))   glue("{round(lp_growth_avg, 2)} percentage points") else "not available"
dom_value_str       <- if (!is.na(dominant_value))  glue("{round(dominant_value, 2)} percentage points") else "not available"
dom_share_str       <- if (!is.na(dominant_share))  percent(dominant_share, accuracy = 1) else "not available"
dom_owner_share_str <- if (!is.na(dominant_owner_share)) percent(dominant_owner_share, accuracy = 0.1) else "not available"

structure_blocks <- c(
  highlight_block("Sector scale and labour footprint",
    glue("{target_sector} contributed **{percent(rev_share_econ, accuracy = 0.1)}** of all-sector revenue and **{percent(lab_share_econ, accuracy = 0.1)}** of employment in {snapshot_year}. It ranked **{ifelse(nrow(sector_ranks_snapshot) == 1, sector_ranks_snapshot$rank_revenue, 'not available')} of 34** by revenue and **{ifelse(nrow(sector_ranks_snapshot) == 1, sector_ranks_snapshot$rank_labour, 'not available')} of 34** by labour, indicating its relative economic scale.")),
  highlight_block("Ownership structure",
    glue("Across 2019–2023, **{dominant_owner}** accounted for **{dom_owner_share_str}** of pooled sector revenue. This ownership profile indicates whether sector growth is primarily domestic-enterprise-led, state-linked or foreign-investment-led.")),
  highlight_block("Investment and adoption diffusion",
    glue("By {snapshot_year}, equipment adoption reached **{percent(adoption_snapshot$equip_rate, accuracy = 0.1)}** of firms and innovation adoption reached **{percent(adoption_snapshot$innov_rate, accuracy = 0.1)}**. Compared with {base_year}, these changed by **{round(equip_rate_change_pp, 1)} percentage points** and **{round(innov_rate_change_pp, 1)} percentage points**, respectively.")),
  highlight_block("Frontier and laggard gap",
    glue("Leading firms were approximately **{frontier_gap_highlight_str}** more productive than laggards in {snapshot_year}. A large ratio points to a capability gap, while a narrowing ratio would suggest stronger diffusion from frontier firms to the wider sector.")),
  highlight_block("Productivity growth engine",
    glue("Average annual labour productivity growth was **{lp_growth_str}** over 2020–2023. The dominant driver was **{dominant_name}**, contributing **{dom_value_str}** (**{dom_share_str}** of total). {diffusion_type_msg} {effi_sign_msg}")),
  highlight_block("Regional technology concentration",
    glue("The highest equipment adoption rate in {snapshot_year} was observed in **{top_equip_province$new_province_name}** at **{percent(top_equip_province$equipment_adoption_rate, accuracy = 0.1)}**, while the highest innovation adoption rate was in **{top_innov_province$new_province_name}** at **{percent(top_innov_province$innovation_adoption_rate, accuracy = 0.1)}**.")),
  highlight_block("Frontier gap trend",
    glue("The productivity gap between leading and laggard firms has {frontier_trend_msg}. Whether this gap is narrowing over time is the central diagnostic for whether technology adoption is generating broad-based gains or concentrating productivity at the frontier."))
)

cat('<div class="highlight-grid">')
cat(paste(structure_blocks, collapse = "\n"))
cat('</div>')
```

## 4.2 Policy Implications

<p class="section-desc">
Sector-specific policy diagnosis based on the growth regime, frontier dynamics, diffusion quality, investment mix, ownership structure and regional adoption pattern.
</p>

```{r s4-policy, results='asis'}
diffusion_constraint <- have_decomp && !is.na(diffusion_share) && !is.na(frontier_gap_latest) &&
  frontier_gap_latest > 5 && diffusion_share < 0.30

innovation_constraint <- !is.na(innovation_investment_share) && innovation_investment_share < 0.20 &&
  !is.na(adoption_snapshot$innov_rate) && adoption_snapshot$innov_rate < 0.20

regional_constraint <- !is.na(top_equip_province$equipment_adoption_rate) &&
  !is.na(adoption_snapshot$equip_rate) &&
  top_equip_province$equipment_adoption_rate > adoption_snapshot$equip_rate * 1.5

main_constraint <- case_when(
  diffusion_constraint ~ "diffusion bottleneck",
  innovation_constraint ~ "innovation capability constraint",
  regime_decomp == "capital-led" ~ "capital-heavy growth with capability absorption risk",
  regime_decomp == "frontier-led" ~ "frontier-led growth with spillover risk",
  regime_decomp == "diffusion-led" ~ "diffusion-led growth that requires scaling support",
  regime_decomp == "efficiency-led" ~ "efficiency-led growth with management and allocation constraints",
  regional_constraint ~ "regional diffusion gap",
  TRUE ~ "mixed productivity constraint"
)

policy_priority <- case_when(
  main_constraint == "diffusion bottleneck" ~
    "Policy should prioritise SME technology absorption, supplier-development programmes and technical extension services that help non-frontier firms adopt existing technologies more effectively.",
  main_constraint == "innovation capability constraint" ~
    "Policy should strengthen innovation capability through applied R&D partnerships, digital capability programmes and targeted support for intangible investment rather than relying only on machinery upgrading.",
  main_constraint == "capital-heavy growth with capability absorption risk" ~
    "Policy should avoid relying solely on further capital expansion. The priority is complementary capability building, including workforce skills, management upgrading and operational know-how that raise returns to capital.",
  main_constraint == "frontier-led growth with spillover risk" ~
    "Policy should pair frontier innovation support with mechanisms that transfer capability outward, including supplier linkages, standards, training platforms and demonstration projects for lagging firms.",
  main_constraint == "diffusion-led growth that requires scaling support" ~
    "Policy should reinforce the existing diffusion channel by expanding access to investment finance, digital adoption support and regional technical assistance for smaller firms.",
  main_constraint == "efficiency-led growth with management and allocation constraints" ~
    "Policy should focus on management modernisation, competition pressure and resource-allocation improvements that help firms convert existing inputs into higher output.",
  main_constraint == "regional diffusion gap" ~
    "Policy should address regional unevenness by targeting provinces where technology adoption remains low, while using leading provinces as demonstration hubs for capability diffusion.",
  TRUE ~
    "Policy should use a balanced package of capability building, diffusion support and selective frontier upgrading, because no single constraint dominates the sector's productivity pattern."
)

# ── C3: Creation vs adoption gap framing ──────────────────────────────────────
creation_share <- if (have_decomp && !is.na(decomp_avg$g_tech_change) && !is.na(decomp_avg$gLP_check))
  safe_divide(decomp_avg$g_tech_change, decomp_avg$gLP_check) else NA_real_

adoption_gap_framing <- case_when(
  is.na(creation_share) | is.na(diffusion_share) ~ "",
  creation_share < 0.10 & diffusion_share < 0.30 ~
    " Both frontier advancement and adoption diffusion are constrained : the sector faces a dual gap where neither the creation of new technological capacity nor the spread of existing practices is generating strong momentum.",
  creation_share < 0.10 & diffusion_share >= 0.30 ~
    " Diffusion is the primary productivity engine but frontier creation is limited. Gains depend on spreading existing technology rather than generating new capability , sustainable in the short run but exposed to exhaustion of the adoptable stock.",
  creation_share >= 0.40 & diffusion_share < 0.20 ~
    " A frontier-diffusion imbalance: leading firms are advancing but the broader sector cannot absorb these advances. The bottleneck is diffusion capacity (absorption capability, skills and finance), not the supply of technology.",
  TRUE ~ ""
)

# ── C3: FDI-spillover risk flag ────────────────────────────────────────────────
fdi_spillover_risk <- isTRUE(
  !is.na(dominant_owner_share) &&
  dominant_owner == "FDI" &&
  dominant_owner_share > 0.50 &&
  regime_decomp == "capital-led"
)

ownership_spillover_msg <- if (fdi_spillover_risk) {
  glue(" The sector's FDI-dominated revenue structure ({percent(dominant_owner_share, accuracy = 0.1)} from foreign-invested firms) combined with a capital-led growth regime signals a passive FDI dependence risk: capital accumulation accelerates through foreign investment but domestic firm capability may not be advancing in parallel. Policy should prioritise domestic absorptive capacity, including supplier-linkage programmes, workforce capability platforms and technology extension, alongside any further FDI attraction efforts.")
} else {
  ""
}

# ── Safe display strings for policy box (NA-proof) ───────────────────────────
regime_decomp_str      <- if (have_decomp) regime_decomp else "not available (no decomposition data)"
frontier_gap_str       <- if (!is.na(frontier_gap_latest)) glue("{round(frontier_gap_latest, 1)}x") else "not available"
diffusion_share_str    <- if (!is.na(diffusion_share)) percent(diffusion_share, accuracy = 1) else "not available"
effi_str               <- if (!is.na(effi_value)) glue("{round(effi_value, 2)} percentage points") else "not available"
innov_invest_str       <- if (!is.na(innovation_investment_share)) percent(innovation_investment_share, accuracy = 0.1) else "not available"

cat(glue("
<div class='policy-box'>
<strong>Policy diagnosis.</strong> The evidence points to a <strong>{main_constraint}</strong> in {target_sector}. This diagnosis draws on the sector's <strong>{regime_decomp_str}</strong> decomposition profile, a frontier-laggard ratio of <strong>{frontier_gap_str}</strong>, diffusion's contribution of <strong>{diffusion_share_str}</strong> to growth, efficiency change of <strong>{effi_str}</strong>, and an innovation-investment share of <strong>{innov_invest_str}</strong> in {snapshot_year}.{adoption_gap_framing}
</div>

<div class='policy-box'>
<strong>Immediate policy implication.</strong> {policy_priority} The goal is not simply to raise investment volume, but to improve the mechanisms through which firms convert capital, equipment, innovation and management practices into sustained productivity gains.{ownership_spillover_msg}
</div>

<div class='policy-box'>
<strong>Implementation focus.</strong> For this sector, interventions should be sequenced around firm capability, technology absorption and diffusion quality. Where the frontier gap is widening, priority goes to mechanisms that transfer capability outward (supplier development, training platforms, standards and demonstration projects) rather than expanding investment volume alone. Where efficiency change is negative, management modernisation and operational upgrading offer the fastest near-term productivity dividend without requiring additional capital.
</div>
"))
```

## 4.3 Strategic Closing Statement

```{r s4-closing, results='asis'}
closing_statement <- case_when(
  regime_decomp == "capital-led" ~
    glue("Sustained productivity growth in {target_sector} will require a transition from capital accumulation toward capability upgrading. The sector's capital-led profile suggests diminishing returns to further physical investment unless complemented by improvements in workforce skills, management practice and technology absorption. The long-term competitiveness question is not how much capital is added, but how effectively that capital is converted into output by a wider range of firms. At Vietnam's current development stage, capital-led sectors that do not generate strong domestic capability diffusion risk becoming sources of capital efficiency decline rather than productivity leadership , particularly where FDI integration is high but domestic value-added and supplier linkages remain shallow."),
  regime_decomp == "frontier-led" ~
    glue("Sustained productivity growth in {target_sector} depends on whether frontier firms can act as engines of broader capability diffusion. The frontier-led growth pattern indicates that leading firms are advancing rapidly, but the gap between these firms and the wider sector risks becoming self-reinforcing unless mechanisms for technology transfer, supplier development and workforce mobility are strengthened. Long-term competitiveness requires that frontier gains eventually reach a broader base of producers. Frontier-led sectors carry high potential as national diffusion sources: when leading firms are connected to domestic supply chains, their technology decisions and workforce practices create spillover pathways that policy can reinforce through standards, linkage programmes and technology extension."),
  regime_decomp == "diffusion-led" ~
    glue("Sustained productivity growth in {target_sector} is well-served by its diffusion-led profile, in which capability spreads across the firm distribution rather than concentrating at the frontier. The key long-term risk is that this diffusion channel loses momentum if the stock of adoptable practices becomes exhausted and is not renewed by fresh frontier innovation. Maintaining competitiveness over the longer term will require pairing diffusion support with investments in new technology development. Diffusion-led sectors contribute directly to inclusive growth by spreading productivity gains beyond a narrow frontier ; these sectors are also the most exposed to technology exhaustion if the practices being diffused are not continuously renewed by frontier advancement or international technology transfer."),
  regime_decomp == "efficiency-led" ~
    glue("Sustained productivity growth in {target_sector} has drawn heavily on operational and managerial catch-up. Efficiency-led gains are real but bounded: once the most tractable improvements in resource allocation and management practice have been captured, further growth requires a different engine. The sector's long-term competitiveness depends on whether efficiency gains can be complemented by rising technology intensity and innovation capability. Efficiency-led gains are particularly important for employment quality: operational catch-up tends to improve conditions for the median worker, and sectors on this trajectory offer a path to productivity improvement that is inclusive before it becomes innovation-intensive."),
  regime_decomp == "contraction" ~
    glue("Restoring productivity growth in {target_sector} requires first diagnosing whether the recorded contraction reflects a cyclical shock or a structural shift. Standard productivity levers (diffusion support, capital upgrading, capability programmes) should be sequenced behind that diagnosis, since applying growth-mode policies to a structurally contracting sector risks misallocating resources toward activities that do not address the underlying cause. A priority in this regime is to preserve firm capability and workforce skills during the contraction phase: the absorptive capacity of surviving firms determines how quickly the sector can benefit from new technology investment when conditions stabilise."),
  regime_decomp == "unavailable" ~
    glue("Labour productivity decomposition data is not available for {target_sector} in this dataset. A structural assessment of the sector's growth regime cannot be made without the LP decomposition workbook covering this sector. Policy analysis should treat the structural and diffusion findings on pages 1 and 2 as the primary evidence base until decomposition data becomes available."),
  TRUE ~
    glue("Sustained productivity growth in {target_sector} will depend less on any single driver and more on the quality of interactions between capital accumulation, capability diffusion and operational efficiency. Over the longer term, the sector's competitiveness will increasingly depend on its ability to translate improvements across multiple channels into broad-based capability upgrading, workforce adaptation and more inclusive productivity gains. Sectors with mixed productivity regimes are typically at a structural transition point (neither clearly behind the diffusion frontier nor pulling ahead of it) and the policy priority is to identify the emerging dominant channel and build the institutional capacity to accelerate it before the window for leapfrogging closes.")
)

cat(glue("
<div class='strategy-close'>
{closing_statement}
</div>
"))
```
